## Agent

The authentication logic in `gefifi-2/backend/src/auth.ts` is complete.

Now, I'll implement the API routes in `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts`. We'll start with the authentication endpoints:

- `POST /api/auth/register`
- `POST /api/auth/login`
- `GET /api/auth/me` (protected)
- A placeholder for `POST /api/auth/google`

Here's the code for `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts`:

```typescript
import express, { Router, Request, Response } from 'express';
import { SimpleDB } from './data';
import { User } from './interfaces'; // Assuming User interface is in interfaces.ts
import {
	hashPassword,
	comparePassword,
	generateToken,
	authenticateToken,
	AuthenticatedRequest
	// JwtPayload // Not directly used here but good to remember its structure
} from './auth';
import crypto from 'crypto'; // For generating unique IDs
// For Google Auth (to be implemented more fully later)
// import { OAuth2Client } from 'google-auth-library';
// const GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID = process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID;
// const client = new OAuth2Client(GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID);

// Initialize database for users
// Make sure the User interface matches what SimpleDB expects (e.g., includes 'id')
const usersDB = new SimpleDB<User>('users.json');

const router = Router();

// --- Helper: Validate User Profile based on UserType ---
const validateProfile = (
	profile: any,
	userType: User['userType']
): { valid: boolean; message?: string } => {
	if (!profile || typeof profile !== 'object')
		return { valid: false, message: 'Profile must be an object.' };

	// Basic common fields (optional but good to check type if present)
	if (profile.fullName && typeof profile.fullName !== 'string')
		return { valid: false, message: 'Full name must be a string.' };
	if (profile.phoneNumber && typeof profile.phoneNumber !== 'string')
		return { valid: false, message: 'Phone number must be a string.' };
	if (profile.location && typeof profile.location !== 'string')
		return { valid: false, message: 'Location must be a string.' };

	// UserType specific required fields (example)
	// For a demo, we might keep this loose, but in production, this would be stricter.
	// switch (userType) {
	//     case 'expert':
	//         if (!profile.expertise || typeof profile.expertise !== 'string') {
	//             return { valid: false, message: 'Expertise is required for experts.' };
	//         }
	//         break;
	//     case 'supplier':
	//         if (!profile.companyName || typeof profile.companyName !== 'string') {
	//             return { valid: false, message: 'Company name is required for suppliers.' };
	//         }
	//         break;
	// }
	return { valid: true };
};

// --- Authentication Routes ---

// POST /api/auth/register
router.post('/auth/register', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
	try {
		const { email, password, userType, profile } = req.body;

		// Basic validation
		if (!email || !password || !userType) {
			return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email, password, and userType are required.' });
		}
		if (typeof email !== 'string' || !email.includes('@')) {
			return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Valid email is required.' });
		}
		if (typeof password !== 'string' || password.length < 6) {
			return res
				.status(400)
				.json({ message: 'Password must be a string and at least 6 characters long.' });
		}
		if (!['customer', 'expert', 'supplier'].includes(userType)) {
			return res
				.status(400)
				.json({ message: 'Invalid userType. Must be customer, expert, or supplier.' });
		}

		const profileValidation = validateProfile(profile, userType);
		if (!profileValidation.valid) {
			return res
				.status(400)
				.json({ message: profileValidation.message || 'Invalid profile data.' });
		}

		const existingUsers = await usersDB.getAll();
		if (existingUsers.some((u) => u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase())) {
			return res.status(409).json({ message: 'User with this email already exists.' });
		}

		const hashedPassword = await hashPassword(password);
		const userId = crypto.randomUUID();
		const now = new Date().toISOString();

		const newUser: User = {
			id: userId,
			email: email.toLowerCase(),
			password: hashedPassword,
			userType,
			profile: profile || {},
			createdAt: now,
			updatedAt: now,
			isActive: true // New users are active by default
		};

		const createdUser = await usersDB.create(newUser);
		const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = createdUser; // Exclude password from response

		const token = generateToken({
			id: createdUser.id,
			email: createdUser.email,
			userType: createdUser.userType
		});

		res.status(201).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'User registered successfully.' });
	} catch (error) {
		console.error('Registration error:', error);
		// Check for specific error messages if SimpleDB throws them
		if (error.message && error.message.includes('already exists')) {
			return res.status(409).json({ message: error.message });
		}
		res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during registration.' });
	}
});

// POST /api/auth/login
router.post('/auth/login', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
	try {
		const { email, password } = req.body;

		if (!email || !password) {
			return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password are required.' });
		}
		if (typeof email !== 'string' || typeof password !== 'string') {
			return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password must be strings.' });
		}

		const users = await usersDB.getAll();
		const user = users.find((u) => u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase());

		if (!user || !user.isActive) {
			// Check if user exists and is active
			return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials or user account is inactive.' });
		}
		if (!user.password) {
			// User might be registered via Google, so no password
			return res
				.status(401)
				.json({
					message: 'Login with email/password not available for this account. Try Google Sign-In.'
				});
		}

		const isPasswordMatch = await comparePassword(password, user.password);
		if (!isPasswordMatch) {
			return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials.' });
		}

		const token = generateToken({ id: user.id, email: user.email, userType: user.userType });
		const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user; // Exclude password from response

		res.status(200).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'Login successful.' });
	} catch (error) {
		console.error('Login error:', error);
		res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during login.' });
	}
});

// GET /api/auth/me - Get current authenticated user's details
router.get('/auth/me', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {
	try {
		// req.user is populated by the authenticateToken middleware
		if (!req.user || !req.user.id) {
			// This case should ideally be caught by authenticateToken, but good to double check
			return res
				.status(401)
				.json({ message: 'Authentication token did not provide user details.' });
		}

		const user = await usersDB.findById(req.user.id);
		if (!user || !user.isActive) {
			return res.status(404).json({ message: 'User not found or account is inactive.' });
		}

		const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user; // Exclude password
		res.status(200).json(userToReturn);
	} catch (error) {
		console.error('Error fetching current user (/auth/me):', error);
		res.status(500).json({ message: 'Error fetching user details.' });
	}
});

// POST /api/auth/google - Google Sign-In (Basic Placeholder & Future Implementation Notes)
router.post('/auth/google', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
	const { googleTokenId } = req.body; // Token ID from Google Sign-In on frontend

	if (!googleTokenId) {
		return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Google token ID (googleTokenId) is required.' });
	}

	// --- Full Google Sign-In Implementation would involve: ---
	// 1. Verify `googleTokenId` with Google servers using `google-auth-library`
	//    const ticket = await client.verifyIdToken({
	//        idToken: googleTokenId,
	//        audience: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
	//    });
	//    const payload = ticket.getPayload();
	//    if (!payload || !payload.email || !payload.sub) {
	//        return res.status(400).json({ message: "Invalid Google token or missing email/ID." });
	//    }
	//    const { email, sub: googleId, name, picture } = payload;

	// 2. Check if user with this `googleId` or `email` already exists in your DB
	//    let users = await usersDB.getAll();
	//    let user = users.find(u => u.googleId === googleId || (u.email === email && !u.googleId));

	// 3. If user exists:
	//    - If found by `googleId`, update profile details (name, picture) if necessary.
	//    - If found by `email` but no `googleId`, link the Google account (set `user.googleId = googleId`).
	//    - Generate JWT and log them in.
	//
	// 4. If user does NOT exist:
	//    - Create a new user record with details from Google (email, googleId, profile with name/picture).
	//    - IMPORTANT: You'll need to determine `userType` here.
	//      - This is a crucial step for a demo. You might:
	//        a) Ask the user for their type on the frontend AFTER Google sign-in but before completing registration.
	//        b) For the demo, default to 'customer' or have a way to pass it.
	//    - Generate JWT.
	//
	// For this demo, we will simulate finding/creating a user if a 'mockGoogleUser' is passed.

	if (req.body.mockGoogleUser) {
		// Simulated part for easier demo without full Google lib setup now
		const { email, googleId, userType, profile } = req.body.mockGoogleUser;
		if (!email || !googleId || !userType) {
			return res
				.status(400)
				.json({ message: 'Mock Google user requires email, googleId, and userType.' });
		}
		let users = await usersDB.getAll();
		let user = users.find(
			(u) => u.googleId === googleId || u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase()
		);
		const now = new Date().toISOString();

		if (!user) {
			const newUserId = crypto.randomUUID();
			user = {
				id: newUserId,
				email: email.toLowerCase(),
				googleId,
				userType,
				profile: profile || { fullName: email.split('@')[0] }, // Basic profile
				createdAt: now,
				updatedAt: now,
				isActive: true
			};
			await usersDB.create(user);
		} else {
			// If user exists, ensure googleId is set if they previously registered with email
			if (!user.googleId) {
				user.googleId = googleId;
			}
			user.updatedAt = now; // Update timestamp
			await usersDB.update(user.id, {
				googleId: user.googleId,
				profile: user.profile,
				updatedAt: user.updatedAt
			});
		}

		const token = generateToken({ id: user.id, email: user.email, userType: user.userType });
		const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user;
		return res
			.status(200)
			.json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'Google Sign-In successful (simulated).' });
	}

	console.log('Received Google Token ID:', googleTokenId);
	// For non-mocked flow, you'd proceed with actual Google verification
	res
		.status(501)
		.json({
			message:
				'Google Sign-In: Real verification not implemented in this step. Send mockGoogleUser for demo.'
		});
});

console.log('API routes module (routes.ts) loaded.');
export default router;
```

```diff
+import express, { Router, Request, Response } from 'express';
+import { SimpleDB, Identifiable } from './data'; // Assuming Identifiable is exported from data.ts
+import { User, UserProfile } from './interfaces';
+import {
+  hashPassword,
+  comparePassword,
+  generateToken,
+  authenticateToken,
+  AuthenticatedRequest,
+  JwtPayload
+} from './auth';
+import crypto from 'crypto'; // For generating unique IDs
+
+// For Google Auth (actual library not fully integrated in this step)
+// import { OAuth2Client } from 'google-auth-library';
+// const GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID = process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID;
+// const googleAuthClient = GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID ? new OAuth2Client(GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID) : null;
+
+// Initialize database for users
+const usersDB = new SimpleDB<User>('users.json');
+
+const router = Router();
+
+// --- Helper: Validate User Profile based on UserType ---
+const validateProfileData = (profile: any, userType: User['userType']): { valid: boolean, message?: string, validatedProfile: UserProfile } => {
+    if (!profile || typeof profile !== 'object') {
+        // For demo, if no profile is sent, we can create an empty one.
+        // In production, you might require it or specific fields.
+        return { valid: true, validatedProfile: {} };
+    }
+
+    const validatedProfile: UserProfile = {};
+
+    // Common fields
+    if (profile.fullName && typeof profile.fullName === 'string') validatedProfile.fullName = profile.fullName;
+    if (profile.phoneNumber && typeof profile.phoneNumber === 'string') validatedProfile.phoneNumber = profile.phoneNumber;
+    if (profile.location && typeof profile.location === 'string') validatedProfile.location = profile.location;
+    if (profile.avatarUrl && typeof profile.avatarUrl === 'string') validatedProfile.avatarUrl = profile.avatarUrl;
+
+    // UserType specific fields (examples, can be expanded)
+    switch (userType) {
+        case 'expert':
+            if (profile.expertise && typeof profile.expertise === 'string') validatedProfile.expertise = profile.expertise;
+            if (profile.experience && typeof profile.experience === 'string') validatedProfile.experience = profile.experience;
+            break;
+        case 'supplier':
+            if (profile.companyName && typeof profile.companyName === 'string') validatedProfile.companyName = profile.companyName;
+            if (profile.category && typeof profile.category === 'string') validatedProfile.category = profile.category;
+            if (profile.experience && typeof profile.experience === 'string') validatedProfile.experience = profile.experience;
+            break;
+        case 'customer':
+            // No specific extra fields for customer profile in this basic setup
+            break;
+        default:
+            return { valid: false, message: 'Invalid userType for profile validation.', validatedProfile };
+    }
+    return { valid: true, validatedProfile };
+};
+
+
+// --- Authentication Routes ---
+
+// POST /api/auth/register
+router.post('/auth/register', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
+  try {
+    const { email, password, userType, profile } = req.body;
+
+    // Basic validation
+    if (!email || !password || !userType) {
+      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email, password, and userType are required.' });
+    }
+    if (typeof email !== 'string' || !email.includes('@')) {
+        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'A valid email is required.' });
+    }
+    if (typeof password !== 'string' || password.length < 6) {
+      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must be at least 6 characters long.' });
+    }
+    if (!['customer', 'expert', 'supplier'].includes(userType)) {
+      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid userType. Must be customer, expert, or supplier.' });
+    }
+
+    const profileValidation = validateProfileData(profile, userType);
+    if (!profileValidation.valid) {
+        return res.status(400).json({ message: profileValidation.message || 'Invalid profile data.' });
+    }
+
+    const existingUsers = await usersDB.getAll();
+    if (existingUsers.some(u => u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase())) {
+      return res.status(409).json({ message: 'User with this email already exists. Please login or use a different email.' });
+    }
+
+    const hashedPassword = await hashPassword(password);
+    const userId = crypto.randomUUID();
+    const now = new Date().toISOString();
+
+    const newUser: User = {
+      id: userId,
+      email: email.toLowerCase(),
+      password: hashedPassword,
+      userType,
+      profile: profileValidation.validatedProfile,
+      createdAt: now,
+      updatedAt: now,
+      isActive: true, // New users are active by default
+    };
+
+    const createdUser = await usersDB.create(newUser);
+    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
+    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = createdUser; // Exclude password from response
+
+    const token = generateToken({ id: createdUser.id, email: createdUser.email, userType: createdUser.userType });
+
+    res.status(201).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'User registered successfully.' });
+
+  } catch (error: any) {
+    console.error('Registration error:', error);
+    if (error.message && error.message.includes('already exists')) {
+        return res.status(409).json({ message: error.message });
+    }
+    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during registration.', error: error.message });
+  }
+});
+
+// POST /api/auth/login
+router.post('/auth/login', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
+  try {
+    const { email, password } = req.body;
+
+    if (!email || !password) {
+      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password are required.' });
+    }
+    if (typeof email !== 'string' || typeof password !== 'string') {
+        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password must be strings.' });
+    }
+
+    const users = await usersDB.getAll();
+    const user = users.find(u => u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase());
+
+    if (!user) {
+      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials. User not found.' });
+    }
+    if (!user.isActive) {
+      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Account is inactive. Please contact support.' });
+    }
+    if (!user.password) { // User might be registered via Google, so no password
+        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Login with email/password not available for this account. Try Google Sign-In or reset password (if feature exists).' });
+    }
+
+    const isPasswordMatch = await comparePassword(password, user.password);
+    if (!isPasswordMatch) {
+      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials. Password incorrect.' });
+    }
+
+    const token = generateToken({ id: user.id, email: user.email, userType: user.userType });
+    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
+    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user; // Exclude password from response
+
+    res.status(200).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'Login successful.' });
+
+  } catch (error: any) {
+    console.error('Login error:', error);
+    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during login.', error: error.message });
+  }
+});
+
+// GET /api/auth/me - Get current authenticated user's details
+router.get('/auth/me', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {
+  try {
+    const jwtPayload = req.user as JwtPayload; // Asserting req.user is JwtPayload from authenticateToken
+    if (!jwtPayload || !jwtPayload.id) {
+      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication token did not provide valid user details.' });
+    }
+
+    const user = await usersDB.findById(jwtPayload.id);
+    if (!user) {
+      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'User not found based on token.' });
+    }
+    if (!user.isActive) {
+      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'User account is inactive.' });
+    }
+
+    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
+    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user; // Exclude password
+    res.status(200).json(userToReturn);
+
+  } catch (error: any) {
+    console.error('Error fetching current user (/auth/me):', error);
+    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Error fetching user details.', error: error.message });
+  }
+});
+
+// POST /api/auth/google - Google Sign-In (Basic Placeholder & Future Implementation Notes)
+router.post('/auth/google', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
+    const { googleTokenId, userTypeForNewGoogleUser, profileForNewGoogleUser } = req.body;
+
+    // For a real implementation:
+    // if (!googleAuthClient) {
+    //    return res.status(503).json({ message: "Google Sign-In is not configured on the server." });
+    // }
+    // if (!googleTokenId) {
+    //     return res.status(400).json({ message: "Google token ID (googleTokenId) is required." });
+    // }
+    // try {
+    //     const ticket = await googleAuthClient.verifyIdToken({
+    //         idToken: googleTokenId,
+    //         audience: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
+    //     });
+    //     const payload = ticket.getPayload();
+    //     if (!payload || !payload.email || !payload.sub) {
+    //         return res.status(400).json({ message: "Invalid Google token or missing critical information." });
+    //     }
+    //     const { email, sub: googleId, name, picture } = payload;
+    //     // ... proceed to find or create user ...
+    // } catch (googleError) {
+    //     console.error("Google token verification failed:", googleError);
+    //     return res.status(401).json({ message: "Google token verification failed."});
+    // }
+
+    // --- MOCK Implementation for Demo ---
+    // This part allows simulating Google Sign-In without needing a live Google Client ID during early dev.
+    // The frontend would send a `mockGoogleUser` object instead of `googleTokenId`.
+    if (req.body.mockGoogleUser) {
+        const { email, googleId, name, userType, profile } = req.body.mockGoogleUser;
+        if (!email || !googleId || !userType) {
+            return res.status(400).json({ message: "Mock Google user requires email, googleId, and userType."});
+        }
+        if (!['customer', 'expert', 'supplier'].includes(userType)) {
+            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid userType for mock Google user.' });
+        }
+
+        try {
+            let users = await usersDB.getAll();
+            let user = users.find(u => u.googleId === googleId || u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase());
+            const now = new Date().toISOString();
+            const profileValidation = validateProfileData(profile || { fullName: name }, userType); // Use name from Google if profile not given
+
+            if (!user) { // New user via Google
+                const newUserId = crypto.randomUUID();
+                user = {
+                    id: newUserId,
+                    email: email.toLowerCase(),
+                    googleId,
+                    userType,
+                    profile: profileValidation.validatedProfile,
+                    createdAt: now,
+                    updatedAt: now,
+                    isActive: true,
+                    // No password for Google-only sign-up
+                };
+                await usersDB.create(user);
+            } else { // Existing user
+                if (!user.googleId) user.googleId = googleId; // Link Google ID if not present
+                if (!user.isActive) return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Account is inactive.' });
+                user.userType = user.userType || userType; // Update userType if it was missing
+                user.profile = { ...user.profile, ...profileValidation.validatedProfile }; // Merge profiles
+                user.updatedAt = now;
+                await usersDB.update(user.id, {
+                    googleId: user.googleId,
+                    userType: user.userType,
+                    profile: user.profile,
+                    updatedAt: user.updatedAt
+                });
+            }
+
+            const token = generateToken({ id: user.id, email: user.email, userType: user.userType });
+            // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
+            const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user;
+            return res.status(200).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'Google Sign-In successful (simulated).' });
+
+        } catch (error: any) {
+            console.error("Error during mock Google Sign-In:", error);
+            return res.status(500).json({ message: "Server error during mock Google Sign-In.", error: error.message });
+        }
+    }
+    // --- End MOCK Implementation ---
+
+
+    // If not using mock, and real Google flow is intended but not fully set up:
+    console.log("Received Google Token ID for real verification (not fully implemented):", googleTokenId);
+    res.status(501).json({
+        message: 'Google Sign-In: Real verification not fully implemented in this step. Send `mockGoogleUser` object for demo flow.',
+        note: 'For new Google users via real flow, frontend needs to provide `userTypeForNewGoogleUser` and optionally `profileForNewGoogleUser` if not derivable from Google payload.',
+    });
+});
+
+
+console.log('API routes module (routes.ts) initialized.');
+export default router;

```

'supplier'].includes(userType)) {\n      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid userType. Must be customer, expert, or supplier.' });\n    }\n\n    const profileValidation = validateProfileData(profile, userType);\n    if (!profileValidation.valid) {\n        return res.status(400).json({ message: profileValidation.message || 'Invalid profile data.' });\n    }\n\n    const existingUsers = await usersDB.getAll();\n    if (existingUsers.some(u => u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase())) {\n      return res.status(409).json({ message: 'User with this email already exists. Please login or use a different email.' });\n    }\n\n    const hashedPassword = await hashPassword(password);\n    const userId = crypto.randomUUID();\n    const now = new Date().toISOString();\n\n    const newUser: User = {\n      id: userId,\n      email: email.toLowerCase(),\n      password: hashedPassword,\n      userType,\n      profile: profileValidation.validatedProfile,\n      createdAt: now,\n      updatedAt: now,\n      isActive: true, // New users are active by default\n    };\n\n    const createdUser = await usersDB.create(newUser);\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = createdUser; // Exclude password from response\n    \n    const token = generateToken({ id: createdUser.id, email: createdUser.email, userType: createdUser.userType });\n\n    res.status(201).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'User registered successfully.' });\n\n  } catch (error: any) {\n    console.error('Registration error:', error);\n    if (error.message && error.message.includes('already exists')) {\n        return res.status(409).json({ message: error.message });\n    }\n    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during registration.', error: error.message });\n  }\n});\n\n// POST /api/auth/login\nrouter.post('/auth/login', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {\n  try {\n    const { email, password } = req.body;\n\n    if (!email || !password) {\n      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password are required.' });\n    }\n    if (typeof email !== 'string' || typeof password !== 'string') {\n        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password must be strings.' });\n    }\n\n    const users = await usersDB.getAll();\n    const user = users.find(u => u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase());\n\n    if (!user) {\n      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials. User not found.' });\n    }\n    if (!user.isActive) {\n      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Account is inactive. Please contact support.' });\n    }\n    if (!user.password) { // User might be registered via Google, so no password\n        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Login with email/password not available for this account. Try Google Sign-In or reset password (if feature exists).' });\n    }\n\n    const isPasswordMatch = await comparePassword(password, user.password);\n    if (!isPasswordMatch) {\n      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials. Password incorrect.' });\n    }\n\n    const token = generateToken({ id: user.id, email: user.email, userType: user.userType });\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user; // Exclude password from response\n\n    res.status(200).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'Login successful.' });\n\n  } catch (error: any) {\n    console.error('Login error:', error);\n    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during login.', error: error.message });\n  }\n});\n\n// GET /api/auth/me - Get current authenticated user's details\nrouter.get('/auth/me', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n  try {\n    const jwtPayload = req.user as JwtPayload; // Asserting req.user is JwtPayload from authenticateToken\n    if (!jwtPayload || !jwtPayload.id) {\n      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication token did not provide valid user details.' });\n    }\n\n    const user = await usersDB.findById(jwtPayload.id);\n    if (!user) {\n      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'User not found based on token.' });\n    }\n    if (!user.isActive) {\n      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'User account is inactive.' });\n    }\n\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user; // Exclude password\n    res.status(200).json(userToReturn);\n\n  } catch (error: any) {\n    console.error('Error fetching current user (/auth/me):', error);\n    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Error fetching user details.', error: error.message });\n  }\n});\n\n// POST /api/auth/google - Google Sign-In (Basic Placeholder & Future Implementation Notes)\nrouter.post('/auth/google', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {\n    const { googleTokenId, userTypeForNewGoogleUser, profileForNewGoogleUser } = req.body; \n\n    // For a real implementation:\n    // if (!googleAuthClient) {\n    //    return res.status(503).json({ message: \"Google Sign-In is not configured on the server.\" });\n    // }\n    // if (!googleTokenId) {\n    //     return res.status(400).json({ message: \"Google token ID (googleTokenId) is required.\" });\n    // }\n    // try {\n    //     const ticket = await googleAuthClient.verifyIdToken({\n    //         idToken:   return res.status(400).json({ message: \"Invalid Google token or missing critical information.\" });\n    //     }\n    //     const { email, sub: googleId, name, picture } = payload;\n    //     // ... proceed to find or create user ...\n    // } catch (googleError) {\n    //     console.error(\"Google token verification failed:\", googleError);\n    //     return res.status(401).json({ message: \"Google token verification failed.\"});\n    // }\n\n    // --- MOCK Implementation for Demo ---\n    // This part allows simulating Google Sign-In without needing a live Google Client ID during early dev.\n    // The frontend would send a `mockGoogleUser` object instead of `googleTokenId`.\n    if (req.body.mockGoogleUser) {\n        const { email, googleId, name, userType, profile } = req.body.mockGoogleUser;\n        if (!email || !googleId || !userType) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: \"Mock Google user requires email, googleId, and userType.\"});\n        }\n        if (!['customer', 'expert', 'supplier'].includes(userType)) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid userType for mock Google user.' });\n        }\n\n        try {\n            let users = await usersDB.getAll();\n            let user = users.find(u => u.googleId === googleId || u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase());\n            const now = new Date().toISOString();\n            const profileValidation = validateProfileData(profile || { fullName: name }, userType); // Use name from Google if profile not given\n\n            if (!user) { // New user via Google\n                const newUserId = crypto.randomUUID();\n                user = {\n                    id: newUserId,\n                    email: email.toLowerCase(),\n                    googleId,\n                    userType,\n                    profile: profileValidation.validatedProfile,\n                    createdAt: now,\n                    updatedAt: now,\n                    isActive: true,\n                    // No password for Google-only sign-up\n                };\n                await usersDB.create(user);\n            } else { // Existing user\n                if (!user.googleId) user.googleId = googleId; // Link Google ID if not present\n                if (!user.isActive) return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Account is inactive.' });\n                user.userType = user.userType || userType; // Update userType if it was missing\n                user.profile = { ...user.profile, ...profileValidation.validatedProfile }; // Merge profiles\n                user.updatedAt = now;\n                await usersDB.update(user.id, { \n                    googleId: user.googleId, \n                    userType: user.userType,\n                    profile: user.profile, \n                    updatedAt: user.updatedAt \n                });\n            }\n            \n            const token = generateToken({ id: user.id, email: user.email, userType: user.userType });\n            // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n            const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user;\n            return res.status(200).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'Google Sign-In successful (simulated).' });\n\n        } catch (error: any) {\n            console.error(\"Error during mock Google ## Agent

The authentication routes (`/register`, `/login`, `/me`, and a mockable `/google`) are now implemented in `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts`. The Google Sign-In route includes a mock flow for easier testing without needing a live Google Client ID setup immediately; you can send a `mockGoogleUser` object in the request body for that.

The next step is to integrate these routes into our main Express application in `gefifi-2/backend/src/server.ts`. This involves:

1.  Importing the `router` from `./routes.ts`.
2.  Using it as middleware for the `/api` path.

I'll read the current `server.ts`, make these changes, and then write it back.

import express, { Express, Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';
import cors from 'cors';
import dotenv from 'dotenv';
import path from 'path';
import multer from 'multer';
import fs from 'fs';

// Load environment variables from .env file in the backend directory
dotenv.config({ path: path.resolve(\_\_dirname, '..', '.env') });

// Placeholder for API routes (will be uncommented later)
// import apiRoutes from './routes';

const app: Express = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

// Middlewares
app.use(cors()); // Enable CORS for all routes
app.use(express.json()); // Parse JSON request bodies
app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: true })); // Parse URL-encoded request bodies

// --- File Upload Setup (Multer) ---
const uploadsDir = path.join(\_\_dirname, '..', 'uploads');

// Ensure uploads directory exists
if (!fs.existsSync(uploadsDir)) {
fs.mkdirSync(uploadsDir, { recursive: true });
console.log(`Created uploads directory at: ${uploadsDir}`);
}

// Set up storage for Multer
const storage = multer.diskStorage({
destination: function (req, file, cb) {
cb(null, uploadsDir); // Files will be saved in the 'uploads' directory
},
filename: function (req, file, cb) {
// Use a timestamp and random number for unique filenames, preserving the original extension
const uniqueSuffix = Date.now() + '-' + Math.round(Math.random() \* 1E9);
cb(null, file.fieldname + '-' + uniqueSuffix + path.extname(file.originalname));
}
});

const upload = multer({
storage: storage,
limits: { fileSize: 10 _ 1024 _ 1024 } // Optional: Limit file size (e.g., 10MB)
});

// Serve static files from the 'uploads' directory, making them accessible via /uploads URL path
app.use('/uploads', express.static(uploadsDir));

// --- Basic Routes ---
app.get('/', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
res.send('GEFIFI Backend Server is running!');
});

// --- API Routes (to be implemented and uncommented from ./routes.ts) ---
// app.use('/api', apiRoutes);

// --- File Upload Endpoint ---
// This endpoint handles single file uploads. 'file' is the field name in the form-data.
app.post('/api/upload', upload.single('file'), (req: Request, res: Response) => {
if (!req.file) {
return res.status(400).json({ message: 'No file uploaded. Please select a file to upload.' });
}
// req.file contains information about the uploaded file (e.g., filename, path, size)
// The file is already saved to the uploadsDir by Multer.
// We return a publicly accessible path to the file.
const relativeFilePath = `/uploads/${req.file.filename}`;
res.status(200).json({
message: 'File uploaded successfully!',
filePath: relativeFilePath, // This path can be used by the frontend to display/access the image
fileName: req.file.filename,
originalName: req.file.originalname,
mimeType: req.file.mimetype,
size: req.file.size
});
});

// --- Error Handling Middleware (Basic) ---
// This should be one of the last middleware functions added.
app.use((err: Error, req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
console.error("Global error handler:", err.stack);
// If the error is from Multer (e.g., file too large)
if (err instanceof multer.MulterError) {
return res.status(400).json({ message: `File upload error: ${err.message}` });
}
// For other errors
res.status(500).json({ message: 'An unexpected error occurred on the server.' });
});

// --- Start Server ---
app.listen(PORT, () => {
console.log(`Server is running on http://localhost:${PORT}`);
console.log(`Attempting to load .env from: ${path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '.env')}`);
console.log(`JWT_SECRET from env: ${process.env.JWT_SECRET ? 'Loaded (first few chars: ' + process.env.JWT_SECRET.substring(0,5) + '...)' : 'NOT Loaded or Empty'}`);
console.log(`Serving uploaded files from static path /uploads, mapped to directory: ${uploadsDir}`);
});

export default app; // Export app for potential serverless deployment or testing

## Agent

Okay, I've reviewed the current `gefifi-2/backend/src/server.ts`. I will now update it to import and use the API router from `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts`.

The changes will be:

1.  Uncomment and modify `// import apiRoutes from './routes';` to `import apiRoutes from './routes';`.
2.  Uncomment and modify `// app.use('/api', apiRoutes);` to `app.use('/api', apiRoutes);`. This will ensure that all routes defined in `routes.ts` are prefixed with `/api`. For example, `/auth/register` in `routes.ts` will become accessible at `/api/auth/register`.

The file upload endpoint `POST /api/upload` is currently defined directly in `server.ts`. It will continue to function as `/api/upload`. For better organization in the future, we might consider moving it into `routes.ts` as well.

Here's the updated content for `gefifi-2/backend/src/server.ts`:

```typescript
import express, { Express, Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';
import cors from 'cors';
import dotenv from 'dotenv';
import path from 'path';
import multer from 'multer';
import fs from 'fs';

// Load environment variables from .env file in the backend directory
dotenv.config({ path: path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '.env') });

// Import API routes
import apiRoutes from './routes';

const app: Express = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

// Middlewares
app.use(cors()); // Enable CORS for all routes
app.use(express.json()); // Parse JSON request bodies
app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: true })); // Parse URL-encoded request bodies

// --- File Upload Setup (Multer) ---
const uploadsDir = path.join(__dirname, '..', 'uploads');

// Ensure uploads directory exists
if (!fs.existsSync(uploadsDir)) {
	fs.mkdirSync(uploadsDir, { recursive: true });
	console.log(`Created uploads directory at: ${uploadsDir}`);
}

// Set up storage for Multer
const storage = multer.diskStorage({
	destination: function (req, file, cb) {
		cb(null, uploadsDir); // Files will be saved in the 'uploads' directory
	},
	filename: function (req, file, cb) {
		// Use a timestamp and random number for unique filenames, preserving the original extension
		const uniqueSuffix = Date.now() + '-' + Math.round(Math.random() * 1e9);
		cb(null, file.fieldname + '-' + uniqueSuffix + path.extname(file.originalname));
	}
});

const upload = multer({
	storage: storage,
	limits: { fileSize: 10 * 1024 * 1024 } // Optional: Limit file size (e.g., 10MB)
});

// Serve static files from the 'uploads' directory, making them accessible via /uploads URL path
app.use('/uploads', express.static(uploadsDir));

// --- Basic Routes ---
app.get('/', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
	res.send('GEFIFI Backend Server is running!');
});

// --- API Routes ---
// Mount the imported API routes from routes.ts under the /api prefix
app.use('/api', apiRoutes);

// --- File Upload Endpoint (still here for now, can be moved to routes.ts later) ---
// This endpoint handles single file uploads. 'file' is the field name in the form-data.
app.post('/api/upload', upload.single('file'), (req: Request, res: Response) => {
	if (!req.file) {
		return res.status(400).json({ message: 'No file uploaded. Please select a file to upload.' });
	}
	const relativeFilePath = `/uploads/${req.file.filename}`;
	res.status(200).json({
		message: 'File uploaded successfully!',
		filePath: relativeFilePath,
		fileName: req.file.filename,
		originalName: req.file.originalname,
		mimeType: req.file.mimetype,
		size: req.file.size
	});
});

// --- Error Handling Middleware (Basic) ---
app.use((err: Error, req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
	console.error('Global error handler:', err.stack);
	if (err instanceof multer.MulterError) {
		return res.status(400).json({ message: `File upload error: ${err.message}` });
	}
	res.status(500).json({ message: 'An unexpected error occurred on the server.' });
});

// --- Start Server ---
app.listen(PORT, () => {
	console.log(`Server is running on http://localhost:${PORT}`);
	console.log(`Attempting to load .env from: ${path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '.env')}`);
	console.log(
		`JWT_SECRET from env: ${process.env.JWT_SECRET ? 'Loaded (first few chars: ' + process.env.JWT_SECRET.substring(0, 5) + '...)' : 'NOT Loaded or Empty'}`
	);
	console.log(
		`Serving uploaded files from static path /uploads, mapped to directory: ${uploadsDir}`
	);
});

export default app;
```

I'll apply this change.

'file' is the field name in the form-data.\napp.post('/api/upload', upload.single('file'), (req: Request, res: Response) => {\n  if (!req.file) {\n    return res.status(400).json({ message: 'No file uploaded. Please select a file to upload.' });\n  }\n  // req.file contains information about the uploaded file (e.g., filename, path, size)\n  // The file is already saved to the uploadsDir by Multer.\n  // We return a publicly accessible path to the file.\n  const relativeFilePath = `/uploads/${req.file.filename}`;\n  res.status(200).json({ \n    message: 'File uploaded successfully!', \n    filePath: relativeFilePath, // This path can be used by the frontend to display/access the image\n    fileName: req.file.filename,\n    originalName: req.file.originalname,\n    mimeType: req.file.mimetype,\n    size: req.file.size\n  });\n});\n\n// --- Error Handling Middleware (Basic) ---\n// This should be one of the last middleware functions added.\napp.use((err: Error, req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {\n  console.error(\"Global error handler:\", err.stack);\n  // If the error is from Multer (e.g., file too large)\n  if (err instanceof multer.MulterError) {\n    return res.status(400).json({ message: `File upload error: ${err.message}` });\n  }\n  // For other errors\n  res.status(500).json({ message: 'An unexpected error occurred on the server.' });\n});\n\n// --- Start Server ---\napp.listen(PORT, () => {\n  console.log(`Server is running on http://localhost:${PORT}`);\n  console.log(`Attempting to load .env from: ${path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '.env')}`);\n  console.log(`JWT_SECRET from env: ${process.env.JWT_SECRET ? 'Loaded (first few chars: ' + process.env.JWT_SECRET.substring(0,5) + '...)' : 'NOT Loaded or Empty'}`);\n  console.log(`Serving uploaded files from static path /uploads, mapped to directory: ${uploadsDir}`);\n});\n\nexport default app; // Export app for potential serverless deployment or testing",
````json
	"old_text": "import express, { Express, Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';\nimport cors from 'cors';\nimport dotenv from 'dotenv';\nimport path from 'path';\nimport multer from 'multer';\nimport fs from 'fs';\n\n// Load environment variables from .env file in the backend directory\ndotenv.config({ path: path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '.env') });\n\n// Placeholder for API routes (will be uncommented later)\n// import apiRoutes from './routes'; \n\nconst app: Express = express();\nconst PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;\n\n// Middlewares\napp.use(cors()); // Enable CORS for all routes\napp.use(express.json()); // Parse JSON request bodies\napp.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: true })); // Parse URL-encoded request bodies\n\n// --- File Upload Setup (Multer) ---\nconst uploadsDir = path.join(__dirname, '..', 'uploads');\n\n// Ensure uploads directory exists\nif (!fs.existsSync(uploadsDir)) {\n  fs.mkdirSync(uploadsDir, { recursive: true });\n  console.log(`Created uploads directory at: ${uploadsDir}`);\n}\n\n// Set up storage for Multer\nconst storage = multer.diskStorage({\n  destination: function (req, file, cb) {\n    cb(null, uploadsDir); // Files will be saved in the 'uploads' directory\n  },\n  filename: function (req, file, cb) {\n    // Use a timestamp and random number for unique filenames, preserving the original extension\n    const uniqueSuffix = Date.now() + '-' + Math.round(Math.random() * 1E9);\n    cb(null, file.fieldname + '-' + uniqueSuffix + path.extname(file.originalname));\n  }\n});\n\nconst upload = multer({ \n  storage: storage,\n  limits: { fileSize: 10 * 1024 * 1024 } // Optional: Limit file size (e.g., 10MB)\n});\n\n// Serve static files from the 'uploads' directory, making them accessible via /uploads URL path\napp.use('/uploads', express.static(uploadsDir));\n\n// --- Basic Routes ---\napp.get('/', (req: Request, res: Response) => {\n  res.send('GEFIFI Backend Server is running!');\n});\n\n// --- API Routes (to be implemented and uncommented from ./routes.ts) ---\n// app.use('/api', apiRoutes); \n\n// --- File Upload Endpoint ---\n// This endpoint handles single file uploads. 'file' is the field name in the form-data.\napp.post('/api/upload', upload.single('file'), (req: Request, res: Response) => {\n  if (!req.file) {\n    return res.status(400).json({ message: 'No file uploaded. Please select a file to upload.' });\n  }\n  // req.file contains information about the uploaded file (e.g., filename, path, size)\n  // The file is already saved to the uploadsDir by Multer.\n  // We return a publicly accessible path to the file.\n  const relativeFilePath = `/uploads/${req.file.filename}`;\n  res.status(200).json({ \n    message: 'File uploaded successfully!', \n    filePath: relativeFilePath, // This path can be used by the frontend to display/access the image\n    fileName: req.file.filename,\n    originalName: req.file.originalname,\n    mimeType: req.file.mimetype,\n    size: req.file.size\n  });\n});\n\n// --- Error Handling Middleware (Basic) ---\n// This should be one of the last middleware functions added.\napp.use((err: Error, req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {\n  console.error(\"Global error handler:\", err.stack);\n  // If the error is from Multer (e.g., file too large)\n  if (err instanceof multer.MulterError) {\n    return res.status(400).json({ message: `File upload error: ${err.message}` });\n  }\n  // For other errors\n  res.status(500).json({ message: 'An unexpected error occurred on the server.' });\n});\n\n// --- Start Server ---\napp.listen(PORT, () => {\n  console.log(`Server is running on http://localhost:${PORT}`);\n  console.log(`Attempting to load .env from: ${path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '.env')}`);\n  console.log(`JWT_SECRET from env: ${process.env.JWT_SECRET ? ## Agent
````

Okay, the backend server in `gefifi-2/backend/src/server.ts` is now updated to use the API routes defined in `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts`. All routes like `/auth/register`, `/auth/login`, etc., will be available under the `/api` prefix (e.g., `/api/auth/register`).

The file upload endpoint `POST /api/upload` remains directly in `server.ts` for now but is functional.

**Summary of Backend Setup So Far:**

- **Project Structure**: `gefifi-2/backend/` with `src/`, `data/`, and `uploads/` directories.
- **Configuration**: `package.json` with dependencies and scripts, and an `.env` file for environment variables.
- **Data Storage (`data.ts`)**: `SimpleDB` class for JSON file-based data storage. It ensures `users.json`, `workRequests.json`, `chats.json`, `messages.json`, and `contracts.json` are created in the `data/` directory.
- **Data Models (`interfaces.ts`)**: TypeScript interfaces for `User`, `WorkRequest`, `Chat`, `Message`, and `Contract`.
- **Authentication Utilities (`auth.ts`)**: Functions for password hashing, JWT generation, and an `authenticateToken` middleware.
- **Server (`server.ts`)**:
  - Express server setup with CORS, body parsing.
  - Multer for file uploads to `uploads/` (accessible via `/uploads/...`).
  - An `/api/upload` endpoint.
  - Integrates all routes from `routes.ts` under the `/api` prefix.
- **API Routes (`routes.ts`)**:
  - `POST /api/auth/register`: Registers a new user (email/password).
  - `POST /api/auth/login`: Logs in a user (email/password).
  - `GET /api/auth/me`: Fetches details of the currently authenticated user (requires JWT).
  - `POST /api/auth/google`: Placeholder for Google Sign-In (currently supports a mock flow for development).

**Next Steps for the Backend:**

1.  **Implement Core Application Endpoints in `routes.ts`**:
    - User-related endpoints: `/users/experts`, `/users/suppliers`, `/users/interest`.
    - Work Request endpoints: `/work-requests`, `/work-requests/:id`.
    - Chat endpoints: `/chat`, `/chat/:id/messages`.
    - Contract endpoints: `/contracts`, `/contracts/:id/sign`.
2.  **Refine Google Sign-In**: If a real Google Client ID is available, the `google-auth-library` can be fully integrated.
3.  **Testing**: It would be good to test these endpoints using a tool like Postman or Insomnia.

For now, the basic authentication and file upload functionalities are in place. You should be able to run the backend server using `bun run dev` from the `gefifi-2/backend/` directory.

What would you like to work on next? We can proceed with implementing the core application endpoints.

Okay, let's proceed with implementing the core application endpoints in `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts`.

We'll tackle these in groups:

1.  **Work Request Endpoints**: For creating, listing, and viewing work requests.
2.  **User List Endpoints**: For getting lists of experts and suppliers.
3.  **Chat Endpoints**: For managing chats and messages.
4.  **Contract Endpoints**: For creating, listing, and signing contracts.
5.  The `/api/users/interest` endpoint will be a bit more involved as it likely ties into chat creation.

First, I need to read the current content of `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts` to append the new routes.

import { Router, Request, Response } from 'express';
import { SimpleDB } from './data'; // Assuming Identifiable is exported from data.ts
import { User, UserProfile } from './interfaces';
import {
hashPassword,
comparePassword,
generateToken,
authenticateToken,
AuthenticatedRequest,
JwtPayload
} from './auth';
import crypto from 'crypto'; // For generating unique IDs

// For Google Auth (actual library not fully integrated in this step)
// import { OAuth2Client } from 'google-auth-library';
// const GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID = process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID;
// const googleAuthClient = GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID ? new OAuth2Client(GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID) : null;

// Initialize database for users
const usersDB = new SimpleDB<User>('users.json');

const router = Router();

// --- Helper: Validate User Profile based on UserType ---
const validateProfileData = (profile: Partial<UserProfile> | null | undefined, userType: User['userType']): { valid: boolean, message?: string, validatedProfile: UserProfile } => {
if (!profile || typeof profile !== 'object') {
// For demo, if no profile is sent, we can create an empty one.
// In production, you might require it or specific fields.
return { valid: true, validatedProfile: {} };
}

    const validatedProfile: UserProfile = {};

    // Common fields
    if (profile.fullName && typeof profile.fullName === 'string') validatedProfile.fullName = profile.fullName;
    if (profile.phoneNumber && typeof profile.phoneNumber === 'string') validatedProfile.phoneNumber = profile.phoneNumber;
    if (profile.location && typeof profile.location === 'string') validatedProfile.location = profile.location;
    if (profile.avatarUrl && typeof profile.avatarUrl === 'string') validatedProfile.avatarUrl = profile.avatarUrl;

    // UserType specific fields (examples, can be expanded)
    switch (userType) {
        case 'expert':
            if (profile.expertise && typeof profile.expertise === 'string') validatedProfile.expertise = profile.expertise;
            if (profile.experience && typeof profile.experience === 'string') validatedProfile.experience = profile.experience;
            break;
        case 'supplier':
            if (profile.companyName && typeof profile.companyName === 'string') validatedProfile.companyName = profile.companyName;
            if (profile.category && typeof profile.category === 'string') validatedProfile.category = profile.category;
            if (profile.experience && typeof profile.experience === 'string') validatedProfile.experience = profile.experience;
            break;
        case 'customer':
            // No specific extra fields for customer profile in this basic setup
            break;
        default:
            return { valid: false, message: 'Invalid userType for profile validation.', validatedProfile };
    }
    return { valid: true, validatedProfile };

};

// --- Authentication Routes ---

// POST /api/auth/register
router.post('/auth/register', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
try {
const { email, password, userType, profile } = req.body;

    // Basic validation
    if (!email || !password || !userType) {
      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email, password, and userType are required.' });
    }
    if (typeof email !== 'string' || !email.includes('@')) {
        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'A valid email is required.' });
    }
    if (typeof password !== 'string' || password.length < 6) {
      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must be at least 6 characters long.' });
    }
    if (!['customer', 'expert', 'supplier'].includes(userType)) {
      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid userType. Must be customer, expert, or supplier.' });
    }

    const profileValidation = validateProfileData(profile, userType);
    if (!profileValidation.valid) {
        return res.status(400).json({ message: profileValidation.message || 'Invalid profile data.' });
    }

    const existingUsers = await usersDB.getAll();
    if (existingUsers.some(u => u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase())) {
      return res.status(409).json({ message: 'User with this email already exists. Please login or use a different email.' });
    }

    const hashedPassword = await hashPassword(password);
    const userId = crypto.randomUUID();
    const now = new Date().toISOString();

    const newUser: User = {
      id: userId,
      email: email.toLowerCase(),
      password: hashedPassword,
      userType,
      profile: profileValidation.validatedProfile,
      createdAt: now,
      updatedAt: now,
      isActive: true, // New users are active by default
    };

    const createdUser = await usersDB.create(newUser);
    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = createdUser; // Exclude password from response

    const token = generateToken({ id: createdUser.id, email: createdUser.email, userType: createdUser.userType });

    res.status(201).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'User registered successfully.' });

} catch (error: unknown) {
console.error('Registration error:', error);
if (error instanceof Error) {
if (error.message && error.message.includes('already exists')) {
return res.status(409).json({ message: error.message });
}
res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during registration.', error: error.message });
} else {
res.status(500).json({ message: 'An unknown error occurred during registration.' });
}
}
});

// POST /api/auth/login
router.post('/auth/login', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
try {
const { email, password } = req.body;

    if (!email || !password) {
      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password are required.' });
    }
    if (typeof email !== 'string' || typeof password !== 'string') {
        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password must be strings.' });
    }

    const users = await usersDB.getAll();
    const user = users.find(u => u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase());

    if (!user) {
      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials. User not found.' });
    }
    if (!user.isActive) {
      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Account is inactive. Please contact support.' });
    }
    if (!user.password) { // User might be registered via Google, so no password
        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Login with email/password not available for this account. Try Google Sign-In or reset password (if feature exists).' });
    }

    const isPasswordMatch = await comparePassword(password, user.password);
    if (!isPasswordMatch) {
      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials. Password incorrect.' });
    }

    const token = generateToken({ id: user.id, email: user.email, userType: user.userType });
    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user; // Exclude password from response

    res.status(200).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'Login successful.' });

} catch (error: unknown) {
console.error('Login error:', error);
if (error instanceof Error) {
res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during login.', error: error.message });
} else {
res.status(500).json({ message: 'An unknown error occurred during login.' });
}
}
});

// GET /api/auth/me - Get current authenticated user's details
router.get('/auth/me', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {
try {
const jwtPayload = req.user as JwtPayload; // Asserting req.user is JwtPayload from authenticateToken
if (!jwtPayload || !jwtPayload.id) {
return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication token did not provide valid user details.' });
}

    const user = await usersDB.findById(jwtPayload.id);
    if (!user) {
      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'User not found based on token.' });
    }
    if (!user.isActive) {
      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'User account is inactive.' });
    }

    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user; // Exclude password
    res.status(200).json(userToReturn);

} catch (error: unknown) {
console.error('Error fetching current user (/auth/me):', error);
if (error instanceof Error) {
res.status(500).json({ message: 'Error fetching user details.', error: error.message });
} else {
res.status(500).json({ message: 'An unknown error occurred fetching user details.' });
}
}
});

// POST /api/auth/google - Google Sign-In (Basic Placeholder & Future Implementation Notes)
router.post('/auth/google', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
const { googleTokenId, \_userTypeForNewGoogleUser, \_profileForNewGoogleUser } = req.body;

    // For a real implementation:
    // if (!googleAuthClient) {
    //    return res.status(503).json({ message: "Google Sign-In is not configured on the server." });
    // }
    // if (!googleTokenId) {
    //     return res.status(400).json({ message: "Google token ID (googleTokenId) is required." });
    // }
    // try {
    //     const ticket = await googleAuthClient.verifyIdToken({
    //         idToken: googleTokenId,
    //         audience: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
    //     });
    //     const payload = ticket.getPayload();
    //     if (!payload || !payload.email || !payload.sub) {
    //         return res.status(400).json({ message: "Invalid Google token or missing critical information." });
    //     }
    //     const { email, sub: googleId, name, picture } = payload;
    //     // ... proceed to find or create user ...
    // } catch (googleError) {
    //     console.error("Google token verification failed:", googleError);
    //     return res.status(401).json({ message: "Google token verification failed."});
    // }

    // --- MOCK Implementation for Demo ---
    // This part allows simulating Google Sign-In without needing a live Google Client ID during early dev.
    // The frontend would send a `mockGoogleUser` object instead of `googleTokenId`.
    if (req.body.mockGoogleUser) {
        const { email, googleId, name, userType, profile } = req.body.mockGoogleUser;
        if (!email || !googleId || !userType) {
            return res.status(400).json({ message: "Mock Google user requires email, googleId, and userType."});
        }
        if (!['customer', 'expert', 'supplier'].includes(userType)) {
            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid userType for mock Google user.' });
        }

        try {
            const users = await usersDB.getAll();
            let user = users.find(u => u.googleId === googleId || u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase());
            const now = new Date().toISOString();
            const profileValidation = validateProfileData(profile || { fullName: name }, userType); // Use name from Google if profile not given

            if (!user) { // New user via Google
                const newUserId = crypto.randomUUID();
                user = {
                    id: newUserId,
                    email: email.toLowerCase(),
                    googleId,
                    userType,
                    profile: profileValidation.validatedProfile,
                    createdAt: now,
                    updatedAt: now,
                    isActive: true,
                    // No password for Google-only sign-up
                };
                await usersDB.create(user);
            } else { // Existing user
                if (!user.googleId) user.googleId = googleId; // Link Google ID if not present
                if (!user.isActive) return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Account is inactive.' });
                user.userType = user.userType || userType; // Update userType if it was missing
                user.profile = { ...user.profile, ...profileValidation.validatedProfile }; // Merge profiles
                user.updatedAt = now;
                await usersDB.update(user.id, {
                    googleId: user.googleId,
                    userType: user.userType,
                    profile: user.profile,
                    updatedAt: user.updatedAt
                });
            }

            const token = generateToken({ id: user.id, email: user.email, userType: user.userType });
            // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
            const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user;
            return res.status(200).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'Google Sign-In successful (simulated).' });

        } catch (error: unknown) {
            console.error("Error during mock Google Sign-In:", error);
            if (error instanceof Error) {
                return res.status(500).json({ message: "Server error during mock Google Sign-In.", error: error.message });
            } else {
                return res.status(500).json({ message: "An unknown server error occurred during mock Google Sign-In." });
            }
        }
    }
    // --- End MOCK Implementation ---


    // If not using mock, and real Google flow is intended but not fully set up:
    console.log("Received Google Token ID for real verification (not fully implemented):", googleTokenId);
    res.status(501).json({
        message: 'Google Sign-In: Real verification not fully implemented in this step. Send `mockGoogleUser` object for demo flow.',
        note: 'For new Google users via real flow, frontend needs to provide `userTypeForNewGoogleUser` and optionally `profileForNewGoogleUser` if not derivable from Google payload.',
    });

});

console.log('API routes module (routes.ts) initialized.');
export default router;

Okay, I'll proceed with implementing the core application endpoints in `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts`. I will build upon the existing content of the file.

Here's the plan:

1.  **Initialize `SimpleDB` Instances**: Add instances for `workRequests.json`, `chats.json`, `messages.json`, and `contracts.json`.
2.  **Implement Work Request Endpoints**:
    - `GET /work-requests`: Get all work requests.
    - `POST /work-requests`: Create a new work request (requires authentication, customer role).
    - `GET /work-requests/:id`: Get a specific work request.
3.  **Implement User List Endpoints**:
    - `GET /users/experts`: Get all users with `userType: 'expert'`.
    - `GET /users/suppliers`: Get all users with `userType: 'supplier'`.

I'll add these incrementally.

Here's the first set of additions to `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts`. This will include the new DB instances and the work request endpoints.

```typescript
import { Router, Request, Response } from 'express';
import { SimpleDB } from './data';
import { User, UserProfile, WorkRequest, Chat, Message, Contract } from './interfaces'; // Added new interfaces
import {
	hashPassword,
	comparePassword,
	generateToken,
	authenticateToken,
	AuthenticatedRequest,
	JwtPayload
} from './auth';
import crypto from 'crypto';

// Initialize databases
const usersDB = new SimpleDB<User>('users.json');
const workRequestsDB = new SimpleDB<WorkRequest>('workRequests.json');
const chatsDB = new SimpleDB<Chat>('chats.json');
const messagesDB = new SimpleDB<Message>('messages.json');
const contractsDB = new SimpleDB<Contract>('contracts.json');

const router = Router();

// --- Helper: Validate User Profile (existing) ---
// ... (existing validateProfileData function) ...

// --- Authentication Routes (existing) ---
// ... (existing /auth/register, /auth/login, /auth/me, /auth/google routes) ...

// --- Work Request Endpoints ---

// GET /api/work-requests - Get all work requests
router.get('/work-requests', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
	try {
		const workRequests = await workRequestsDB.getAll();
		// For a demo, we might sort them by creation date, newest first
		const sortedWorkRequests = workRequests.sort(
			(a, b) => new Date(b.createdAt).getTime() - new Date(a.createdAt).getTime()
		);
		res.status(200).json(sortedWorkRequests);
	} catch (error: any) {
		console.error('Error fetching work requests:', error);
		res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch work requests.', error: error.message });
	}
});

// POST /api/work-requests - Create a new work request
router.post(
	'/work-requests',
	authenticateToken,
	async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {
		try {
			const user = req.user as JwtPayload;
			if (user.userType !== 'customer') {
				return res
					.status(403)
					.json({ message: 'Forbidden. Only customers can create work requests.' });
			}

			const {
				title,
				description,
				images,
				location,
				expectedCost,
				timeline,
				materialsSuggested,
				category
			} = req.body;

			// Basic validation for required fields
			if (!title || !description || !location) {
				return res
					.status(400)
					.json({ message: 'Title, description, and location are required for a work request.' });
			}
			if (images && !Array.isArray(images)) {
				return res
					.status(400)
					.json({ message: 'Images must be an array of strings (file paths).' });
			}
			if (expectedCost && typeof expectedCost !== 'number') {
				return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Expected cost must be a number.' });
			}

			const now = new Date().toISOString();
			const workRequestId = crypto.randomUUID();

			const newWorkRequest: WorkRequest = {
				id: workRequestId,
				customerId: user.id,
				title,
				description,
				images: images || [], // Default to empty array if not provided
				location,
				expectedCost: expectedCost || 0, // Default or handle if not provided
				timeline: timeline || 'Not specified',
				materialsSuggested: materialsSuggested || '',
				status: 'open',
				createdAt: now,
				updatedAt: now,
				category: category || 'General',
				interestedExperts: [],
				interestedSuppliers: []
			};

			const createdWorkRequest = await workRequestsDB.create(newWorkRequest);
			res.status(201).json(createdWorkRequest);
		} catch (error: any) {
			console.error('Error creating work request:', error);
			if (error.message && error.message.includes('already exists')) {
				return res.status(409).json({ message: error.message });
			}
			res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to create work request.', error: error.message });
		}
	}
);

// GET /api/work-requests/:id - Get a specific work request by ID
router.get('/work-requests/:id', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
	try {
		const workRequestId = req.params.id;
		if (!workRequestId) {
			return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Work request ID is required.' });
		}
		const workRequest = await workRequestsDB.findById(workRequestId);
		if (!workRequest) {
			return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Work request not found.' });
		}
		res.status(200).json(workRequest);
	} catch (error: any) {
		console.error(`Error fetching work request ${req.params.id}:`, error);
		res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch work request.', error: error.message });
	}
});

// --- User List Endpoints ---

// GET /api/users/experts - Get all expert users
router.get('/users/experts', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
	try {
		const allUsers = await usersDB.getAll();
		const experts = allUsers.filter((user) => user.userType === 'expert' && user.isActive);
		// Exclude passwords from the response
		const expertsToReturn = experts.map((expert) => {
			const { password, ...expertData } = expert;
			return expertData;
		});
		res.status(200).json(expertsToReturn);
	} catch (error: any) {
		console.error('Error fetching experts:', error);
		res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch experts.', error: error.message });
	}
});

// GET /api/users/suppliers - Get all supplier users
router.get('/users/suppliers', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
	try {
		const allUsers = await usersDB.getAll();
		const suppliers = allUsers.filter((user) => user.userType === 'supplier' && user.isActive);
		// Exclude passwords from the response
		const suppliersToReturn = suppliers.map((supplier) => {
			const { password, ...supplierData } = supplier;
			return supplierData;
		});
		res.status(200).json(suppliersToReturn);
	} catch (error: any) {
		console.error('Error fetching suppliers:', error);
		res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch suppliers.', error: error.message });
	}
});

// (Existing export default router)
```

```json
}
```
