const { participantIds, workRequestId, initialMessageContent } = req.body;\n\n        if (!participantIds || !Array.isArray(participantIds) || participantIds.length === 0) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Participant IDs array is required and must not be empty.' });\n        }\n        const allParticipantIds = [...new Set([user.id, ...participantIds])].sort();\n        if (allParticipantIds.length < 2) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'A chat requires at least two unique participants.' });\n        }\n        for (const pId of allParticipantIds) {\n            const participantUser = await usersDB.findById(pId);\n            if (!participantUser || !participantUser.isActive) {\n                return res.status(404).json({ message: `Participant with ID ${pId} not found or is inactive.` });\n            }\n        }\n        const allChats = await chatsDB.getAll();\n        const existingChat = allChats.find(c => \n            c.participants.length === allParticipantIds.length &&\n            c.participants.every(p => allParticipantIds.includes(p)) &&\n            c.workRequestId === workRequestId\n        );\n        if (existingChat) {\n            return res.status(200).json({ message: 'Chat with these participants (and work request, if specified) already exists.', chat: existingChat });\n        }\n        const now = new Date().toISOString();\n        const chatId = crypto.randomUUID();\n        const newChatData: Chat = {\n            id: chatId,\n            participants: allParticipantIds,\n            workRequestId: workRequestId,\n            createdAt: now,\n            updatedAt: now,\n        };\n        const createdChat = await chatsDB.create(newChatData);\n        if (initialMessageContent && typeof initialMessageContent === 'string' && initialMessageContent.trim() !== '') {\n            const messageId = crypto.randomUUID();\n            const initialMessage: Message = {\n                id: messageId,\n                chatId: createdChat.id,\n                senderId: user.id,\n                content: initialMessageContent,\n                timestamp: now,\n            };\n            await messagesDB.create(initialMessage);\n        }\n        res.status(201).json(createdChat);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error creating chat:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to create chat.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nrouter.post('/chat/:chatId/messages', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const chatId = req.params.chatId;\n        const { content, images } = req.body;\n\n        if ((!content || typeof content !== 'string' || content.trim() === '') && (!images || !Array.isArray(images) || images.length === 0)) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Message content or images are required.' });\n        }\n        if (images && (!Array.isArray(images) || !images.every(img => typeof img === 'string'))) {\n             return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Images must be an array of strings (file paths).' });\n        }\n        const chat = await chatsDB.findById(chatId);\n        if (!chat) {\n            return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Chat not found.' });\n        }\n        if (!chat.participants.includes(user.id)) {\n            return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Forbidden. You are not a participant of this chat.' });\n        }\n        const now = new Date().toISOString();\n        const messageId = crypto.randomUUID();\n        const newMessage: Message = {\n            id: messageId,\n            chatId: chatId,\n            senderId: user.id,\n            content: content || '', \n            images: images || [],\n            timestamp: now,\n        };\n        const createdMessage = await messagesDB.create(newMessage);\n        await chatsDB.update(chatId, { updatedAt: now });\n        res.status(201).json(createdMessage);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error sending message:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to send message.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nrouter.get('/chat/:chatId/messages', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const chatId = req.params.chatId;\n        const chat = await chatsDB.findById(chatId);\n        if (!chat) {\n            return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Chat not found.' });\n        }\n        if (!chat.participants.includes(user.id)) {\n            return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Forbidden. You are not a participant of this chat.' });\n        }\n        const allMessages = await messagesDB.getAll();\n        const chatMessages = allMessages\n            .filter(msg => msg.chatId === chatId)\n            .sort((a, b) => new Date(a.timestamp).getTime() - new Date(b.timestamp).getTime());\n        res.status(200).json(chatMessages);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error(`Error fetching messages for chat ${req.params.chatId}:`, error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch messages.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\n// --- Contract Endpoints ---\nrouter.get('/contracts', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const allContracts = await contractsDB.getAll();\n        const userContracts = allContracts.filter(contract => \n            contract.customerId === user.id || contract.expertSupplierId === user.id\n        );\n        userContracts.sort((a,b) => new Date(b.createdAt).getTime() - new Date(a.createdAt).getTime());\n        res.status(200).json(userContracts);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error fetching contracts:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch contracts.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nrouter.post('/contracts', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const initiator = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const { workRequestId, customerId, expertSupplierId, workDetails, agreementSummary, contractDate } = req.body;\n\n        if (!workRequestId || !customerId || !expertSupplierId || !workDetails || !agreementSummary) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'workRequestId, customerId, expertSupplierId, workDetails, and agreementSummary are required.' });\n        }\n        const workRequest = await workRequestsDB.findById(workRequestId);\n        if (!workRequest) return res.status(404).json({ message: `Work request with ID ${workRequestId} not found.` });\n        if (workRequest.customerId !== customerId) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Mismatch: workRequest.customerId does not match provided customerId.'});\n        }\n        const customer = await usersDB.findById(customerId);\n        const provider = await usersDB.findById(expertSupplierId); \n        if (!customer || !customer.isActive) return res.status(404).json({ message: `Customer with ID ${customerId} not found or inactive.` });\n        if (!provider || !provider.isActive) return res.status(404).json({ message: `Expert/Supplier with ID ${expertSupplierId} not found or inactive.` });\n        if (provider.userType !== 'expert' && provider.userType !== 'supplier') {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'expertSupplierId must belong to an expert or supplier user.' });\n        }\n        if (initiator.id !== customerId && initiator.id !== expertSupplierId) {\n            return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Forbidden. Contract creator must be the customer or the designated expert/supplier.' });\n        }\n        const now = new Date().toISOString();\n        const contractId = crypto.randomUUID();\n        const newContract: Contract = {\n            id: contractId,\n            workRequestId,\n            customerId,\n            expertSupplierId,\n            workDetails,\n            agreementSummary,\n            contractDate: contractDate || now,\n            customerSigned: false,\n            expertSupplierSigned: false,\n            status: 'draft',\n            createdAt: now,\n            updatedAt: now,\n        };\n        const createdContract = await contractsDB.create(newContract);\n        if (workRequest.status === 'in_discussion' || workRequest.status === 'open' || workRequest.status === 'awaiting_quotes') {\n            await workRequestsDB.update(workRequest.id, { status: 'contracted', updatedAt: now });\n        }\n        res.status(201).json(createdContract);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error creating contract:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        if (errorMessage.includes('already exists')) {\n            return res.status(409).json({ message: errorMessage });\n        }\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to create contract.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nrouter.put('/contracts/:contractId/sign', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const contractId = req.params.contractId;\n        const contract = await contractsDB.findById(contractId);\n        if (!contract) {\n            return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Contract not found.' });\n        }\n        if (contract.status !== 'draft' && contract.status !== 'awaiting_signatures') {\n             return res.status(400).json({ message: `Contract in status '${contract.status}' cannot be signed. Must be 'draft' or 'awaiting_signatures'.` });\n        }\n        let signedByCurrentUser = false;\n        const now = new Date().toISOString();\n        const updates: Partial<Contract> = { updatedAt: now };\n\n        if (user.id === contract.customerId) {\n            if (contract.customerSigned) return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Customer has already signed this contract.' });\n            updates.customerSigned = true;\n            updates.customerSignatureTimestamp = now;\n            signedByCurrentUser = true;\n        } else if (user.id === contract.expertSupplierId) {\n            if (contract.expertSupplierSigned) return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Expert/Supplier has already signed this contract.' });\n            updates.expertSupplierSigned = true;\n            updates.expertSupplierSignatureTimestamp = now;\n            signedByCurrentUser = true;\n        } else {\n            return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Forbidden. You are not a party to this contract or cannot sign it.' });\n        }\n\n        if (signedByCurrentUser) {\n            const customerHasSigned = updates.customerSigned || contract.customerSigned;\n            const providerHasSigned = updates.expertSupplierSigned || contract.expertSupplierSigned;\n\n            if (customerHasSigned && providerHasSigned) {\n                updates.status = 'signed';\n            } else {\n                updates.status = 'awaiting_signatures';\n            }\n            const updatedContract = await contractsDB.update(contractId, updates);\n            res.status(200).json(updatedContract);\n        } else {\n            res.status(400).json({ message: 'No signature action was performed. User may have already signed or is not a party.' });\n        }\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error signing contract:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to sign contract.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nconsole.log('API routes module (routes.ts) initialized with core endpoints.');\nexport default router;",
```json
	"old_text": "import { Router, Request, Response } from 'express';\nimport { SimpleDB } from './data';\nimport { User, UserProfile, WorkRequest, Chat, Message, Contract } from './interfaces';\nimport {\n  hashPassword,\n  comparePassword,\n  generateToken,\n  authenticateToken,\n  AuthenticatedRequest,\n  JwtPayload\n} from './auth';\nimport crypto from 'crypto';\n\n// Initialize databases\nconst usersDB = new SimpleDB<User>('users.json');\nconst workRequestsDB = new SimpleDB<WorkRequest>('workRequests.json');\nconst chatsDB = new SimpleDB<Chat>('chats.json');\nconst messagesDB = new SimpleDB<Message>('messages.json');\nconst contractsDB = new SimpleDB<Contract>('contracts.json');\n\nconst router = Router();\n\n// --- Helper: Validate User Profile based on UserType ---\nconst validateProfileData = (profile: Partial<UserProfile> | null | undefined, userType: User['userType']): { valid: boolean, message?: string, validatedProfile: UserProfile } => {\n    if (!profile || typeof profile !== 'object') {\n        return { valid: true, validatedProfile: {} };\n    }\n    \n    const validatedProfile: UserProfile = {};\n\n    if (profile.fullName && typeof profile.fullName === 'string') validatedProfile.fullName = profile.fullName;\n    if (profile.phoneNumber && typeof profile.phoneNumber === 'string') validatedProfile.phoneNumber = profile.phoneNumber;\n    if (profile.location && typeof profile.location === 'string') validatedProfile.location = profile.location;\n    if (profile.avatarUrl && typeof profile.avatarUrl === 'string') validatedProfile.avatarUrl = profile.avatarUrl;\n\n    switch (userType) {\n        case 'expert':\n            if (profile.expertise && typeof profile.expertise === 'string') validatedProfile.expertise = profile.expertise;\n            if (profile.experience && typeof profile.experience === 'string') validatedProfile.experience = profile.experience;\n            break;\n        case 'supplier':\n            if (profile.companyName && typeof profile.companyName === 'string') validatedProfile.companyName = profile.companyName;\n            if (profile.category && typeof profile.category === 'string') validatedProfile.category = profile.category;\n            if (profile.experience && typeof profile.experience === 'string') validatedProfile.experience = profile.experience;\n            break;\n        case 'customer':\n            break;\n        default:\n            return { valid: false, message: 'Invalid userType for profile validation.', validatedProfile };\n    }\n    return { valid: true, validatedProfile };\n};\n\n\n// --- Authentication Routes ---\nrouter.post('/auth/register', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {\n  try {\n    const { email, password, userType, profile } = req.body;\n    if (!email || !password || !userType) {\n      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email, password, and userType are required.' });\n    }\n    if (typeof email !== 'string' || !email.includes('@')) {\n        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'A valid email is required.' });\n    }\n    if (typeof password !== 'string' || password.length < 6) {\n      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Password must be at least 6 characters long.' });\n    }\n    if (!['customer', 'expert', 'supplier'].includes(userType)) {\n      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid userType. Must be customer, expert, or supplier.' });\n    }\n    const profileValidation = validateProfileData(profile, userType);\n    if (!profileValidation.valid) {\n        return res.status(400).json({ message: profileValidation.message || 'Invalid profile data.' });\n    }\n    const existingUsers = await usersDB.getAll();\n    if (existingUsers.some(u => u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase())) {\n      return res.status(409).json({ message: 'User with this email already exists. Please login or use a different email.' });\n    }\n    const hashedPassword = await hashPassword(password);\n    const userId = crypto.randomUUID();\n    const now = new Date().toISOString();\n    const newUser: User = {\n      id: userId,\n      email: email.toLowerCase(),\n      password: hashedPassword,\n      userType,\n      profile: profileValidation.validatedProfile,\n      createdAt: now,\n      updatedAt: now,\n      isActive: true,\n    };\n    const createdUser = await usersDB.create(newUser);\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = createdUser;\n    const token = generateToken({ id: createdUser.id, email: createdUser.email, userType: createdUser.userType });\n    res.status(201).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'User registered successfully.' });\n  } catch (error: unknown) {\n    console.error('Registration error:', error);\n    if (error instanceof Error) {\n        if (error.message && error.message.includes('already exists')) {\n            return res.status(409).json({ message: error.message });\n        }\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during registration.', error: error.message });\n    } else {\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'An unknown error occurred during registration.' });\n    }\n  }\n});\n\nrouter.post('/auth/login', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {\n  try {\n    const { email, password } = req.body;\n    if (!email || !password) {\n      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password are required.' });\n    }\n    if (typeof email !== 'string' || typeof password !== 'string') {\n        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Email and password must be strings.' });\n    }\n    const users = await usersDB.getAll();\n    const user = users.find(u => u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase());\n    if (!user) {\n      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials. User not found.' });\n    }\n    if (!user.isActive) {\n      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Account is inactive. Please contact support.' });\n    }\n    if (!user.password) {\n        return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Login with email/password not available. Try Google Sign-In.' });\n    }\n    const isPasswordMatch = await comparePassword(password, user.password);\n    if (!isPasswordMatch) {\n      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Invalid credentials. Password incorrect.' });\n    }\n    const token = generateToken({ id: user.id, email: user.email, userType: user.userType });\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user;\n    res.status(200).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'Login successful.' });\n  } catch (error: unknown) {\n    console.error('Login error:', error);\n    if (error instanceof Error) {\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error during login.', error: error.message });\n    } else {\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'An unknown error occurred during login.' });\n    }\n  }\n});\n\nrouter.get('/auth/me', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n  try {\n    const jwtPayload = req.user as JwtPayload;\n    if (!jwtPayload || !jwtPayload.id) {\n      return res.status(401).json({ message: 'Authentication token did not provide valid user details.' });\n    }\n    const user = await usersDB.findById(jwtPayload.id);\n    if (!user) {\n      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'User not found based on token.' });\n    }\n    if (!user.isActive) {\n      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'User account is inactive.' });\n    }\n    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n    const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user;\n    res.status(200).json(userToReturn);\n  } catch (error: unknown) {\n    console.error('Error fetching current user (/auth/me):', error);\n     if (error instanceof Error) {\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Error fetching user details.', error: error.message });\n    } else {\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'An unknown error occurred fetching user details.' });\n    }\n  }\n});\n\nrouter.post('/auth/google', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {\n    const { googleTokenId, _userTypeForNewGoogleUser, _profileForNewGoogleUser } = req.body;\n    if (req.body.mockGoogleUser) {\n        const { email, googleId, name, userType, profile } = req.body.mockGoogleUser;\n        if (!email || !googleId || !userType) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: \"Mock Google user requires email, googleId, and userType.\"});\n        }\n        if (!['customer', 'expert', 'supplier'].includes(userType)) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Invalid userType for mock Google user.' });\n        }\n        try {\n            const users = await usersDB.getAll();\n            let user = users.find(u => u.googleId === googleId || u.email.toLowerCase() === email.toLowerCase());\n            const now = new Date().toISOString();\n            const profileValidation = validateProfileData(profile || { fullName: name }, userType);\n            if (!user) {\n                const newUserId = crypto.randomUUID();\n                user = {\n                    id: newUserId,\n                    email: email.toLowerCase(),\n                    googleId,\n                    userType,\n                    profile: profileValidation.validatedProfile,\n                    createdAt: now,\n                    updatedAt: now,\n                    isActive: true,\n                };\n                await usersDB.create(user);\n            } else {\n                if (!user.googleId) user.googleId = googleId;\n                if (!user.isActive) return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Account is inactive.' });\n                user.userType = user.userType || userType;\n                user.profile = { ...user.profile, ...profileValidation.validatedProfile };\n                user.updatedAt = now;\n                await usersDB.update(user.id, {\n                    googleId: user.googleId,\n                    userType: user.userType,\n                    profile: user.profile,\n                    updatedAt: user.updatedAt\n                });\n            }\n            const token = generateToken({ id: user.id, email: user.email, userType: user.userType });\n            // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n            const { password: _, ...userToReturn } = user;\n            return res.status(200).json({ user: userToReturn, token, message: 'Google Sign-In successful (simulated).\\' });\n        } catch (error: unknown) {\n            console.error(\"Error during mock Google Sign-In:\", error);\n            if (error instanceof Error) {\n                return res.status(500).json({ message: \"Server error during mock Google Sign-In.\", error: error.message });\n            } else {\n                return res.status(500).json({ message: \"An unknown server error occurred during mock Google Sign-In.\" });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    console.log(\"Received Google Token ID for real verification (not fully implemented):\", googleTokenId);\n    res.status(501).json({\n        message: 'Google Sign-In: Real verification not fully implemented. Send `mockGoogleUser` object for demo flow.',\n        note: 'For new Google users via real flow, frontend needs to provide `userTypeForNewGoogleUser` and `profileForNewGoogleUser`.',\n    });\n});\n\n// --- Work Request Endpoints ---\nrouter.get('/work-requests', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {\n  try {\n    const workRequests = await workRequestsDB.getAll();\n    const sortedWorkRequests = workRequests.sort((a, b) => new Date(b.createdAt).getTime() - new Date(a.createdAt).getTime());\n    res.status(200).json(sortedWorkRequests);\n  } catch (error: unknown) {\n    console.error('Error fetching work requests:', error);\n    const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch work requests.', error: errorMessage });\n  }\n});\n\nrouter.post('/work-requests', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n  try {\n    const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n    if (user.userType !== 'customer') {\n      return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Forbidden. Only customers can create work requests.' });\n    }\n    const { title, description, images, location, expectedCost, timeline, materialsSuggested, category } = req.body;\n    if (!title || !description || !location) {\n      return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Title, description, and location are required.' });\n    }\n    if (images && (!Array.isArray(images) || !images.every(img => typeof img === 'string'))) {\n        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Images must be an array of strings (file paths).' });\n    }\n    if (expectedCost !== undefined && typeof expectedCost !== 'number') {\n        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Expected cost must be a number if provided.' });\n    }\n    const now = new Date().toISOString();\n    const workRequestId = crypto.randomUUID();\n    const newWorkRequest: WorkRequest = {\n      id: workRequestId,\n      customerId: user.id,\n      title,\n      description,\n      images: images || [],\n      location,\n      expectedCost: expectedCost,\n      timeline: timeline || 'Not specified',\n      materialsSuggested: materialsSuggested || '',\n      status: 'open',\n      createdAt: now,\n      updatedAt: now,\n      category: category || 'General',\n      interestedExperts: [],\n      interestedSuppliers: []\n    };\n    const createdWorkRequest = await workRequestsDB.create(newWorkRequest);\n    res.status(201).json(createdWorkRequest);\n  } catch (error: unknown) {\n    console.error('Error creating work request:', error);\n    const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n    if (errorMessage.includes('already exists')) {\n        return res.status(409).json({ message: errorMessage });\n    }\n    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to create work request.', error: errorMessage });\n  }\n});\n\nrouter.get('/work-requests/:id', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {\n  try {\n    const workRequestId = req.params.id;\n    if (!workRequestId) {\n        return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Work request ID parameter is required.' });\n    }\n    const workRequest = await workRequestsDB.findById(workRequestId);\n    if (!workRequest) {\n      return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Work request not found.' });\n    }\n    res.status(200).json(workRequest);\n  } catch (error: unknown) {\n    console.error(`Error fetching work request ${req.params.id}:`, error);\n    const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch work request.', error: errorMessage });\n  }\n});\n\n// --- User List Endpoints ---\nrouter.get('/users/experts', async (_req: Request, res: Response) => {\n  try {\n    const allUsers = await usersDB.getAll();\n    const experts = allUsers\n      .filter(user => user.userType === 'expert' && user.isActive)\n      .map(expert => {\n        // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n        const { password, ...expertData } = expert;\n        return expertData;\n      });\n    res.status(200).json(experts);\n  } catch (error: unknown) {\n    console.error('Error fetching experts:', error);\n    const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch experts.', error: errorMessage });\n  }\n});\n\nrouter.get('/users/suppliers', async (_req: Request, res: Response) => {\n  try {\n    const allUsers = await usersDB.getAll();\n    const suppliers = allUsers\n      .filter(user => user.userType === 'supplier' && user.isActive)\n      .map(supplier => {\n        // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n        const { password, ...supplierData } = supplier;\n        return supplierData;\n      });\n    res.status(200).json(suppliers);\n  } catch (error: unknown) {\n    console.error('Error fetching suppliers:', error);\n    const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch suppliers.', error: errorMessage });\n  }\n});\n\n// --- User Interest Endpoint (Creates Chat & Initial Message) ---\nrouter.post('/users/interest', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const sender = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const { targetUserId, workRequestId, predefinedMessageKey } = req.body;\n\n        if (!targetUserId) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'targetUserId is required.' });\n        }\n        const targetUser = await usersDB.findById(targetUserId);\n        if (!targetUser || !targetUser.isActive) {\n            return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Target user not found or is inactive.' });\n        }\n        if (sender.id === targetUserId) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Cannot express interest in oneself.' });\n        }\n\n        let workRequest: WorkRequest | undefined;\n        if (workRequestId) {\n            workRequest = await workRequestsDB.findById(workRequestId);\n            if (!workRequest) {\n                return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Associated work request not found.' });\n            }\n        }\n\n        let initialMessageContent = \"Hi, I'm interested in connecting.\";\n        if (predefinedMessageKey === 'CUSTOMER_INTEREST_IN_PROVIDER' && targetUser.profile.fullName) {\n             initialMessageContent = `Hi ${targetUser.profile.fullName || targetUser.email.split('@')[0]}, I'm interested in your services.`;\n        } else if (predefinedMessageKey === 'PROVIDER_INTEREST_IN_WORK_REQUEST' && workRequest) {\n            initialMessageContent = `Hi, I saw your work request \"${workRequest.title}\" and I'm interested in discussing it further.`;\n        }\n\n        const allChats = await chatsDB.getAll();\n        let existingChat = allChats.find(chat =>\n            chat.participants.includes(sender.id) &&\n            chat.participants.includes(targetUserId) &&\n            (!workRequestId || chat.workRequestId === workRequestId)\n        );\n        \n        const now = new Date().toISOString();\n\n        if (!existingChat) {\n            const chatId = crypto.randomUUID();\n            const newChat: Chat = {\n                id: chatId,\n                participants: [sender.id, targetUserId].sort(),\n                workRequestId: workRequestId,\n                createdAt: now,\n                updatedAt: now,\n            };\n            existingChat = await chatsDB.create(newChat);\n        }\n\n        const messageId = crypto.randomUUID();\n        const newMessage: Message = {\n            id: messageId,\n            chatId: existingChat.id,\n            senderId: sender.id,\n            content: initialMessageContent,\n            timestamp: now,\n        };\n        await messagesDB.create(newMessage);\n        existingChat.updatedAt = now;\n        await chatsDB.update(existingChat.id, { updatedAt: now });\n\n        if (workRequest) {\n            if (sender.userType === 'expert') {\n                const wrExperts = workRequest.interestedExperts || [];\n                if (!wrExperts.includes(sender.id)) {\n                    await workRequestsDB.update(workRequest.id, { interestedExperts: [...wrExperts, sender.id] });\n                }\n            } else if (sender.userType === 'supplier') {\n                 const wrSuppliers = workRequest.interestedSuppliers || [];\n                 if (!wrSuppliers.includes(sender.id)) {\n                    await workRequestsDB.update(workRequest.id, { interestedSuppliers: [...wrSuppliers, sender.id] });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        \n        res.status(201).json({ \n            message: 'Interest expressed successfully. Chat initiated/updated.', \n            chatId: existingChat.id,\n            initialMessage: newMessage \n        });\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error expressing interest:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to express interest.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\n// --- Chat Endpoints ---\nrouter.get('/chat', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const allChats = await chatsDB.getAll();\n        const userChats = allChats.filter(chat => chat.participants.includes(user.id));\n        userChats.sort((a,b) => new Date(b.updatedAt || b.createdAt).getTime() - new Date(a.updatedAt || a.createdAt).getTime());\n        res.status(200).json(userChats);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error fetching user chats:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch chats.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nrouter.post('/chat', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const { participantIds, workRequestId, initialMessageContent } = req.body;\n\n        if (!participantIds || !Array.isArray(participantIds) || participantIds.length === 0) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Participant IDs array is required and must not be empty.' });\n        }\n        const allParticipantIds = [...new Set([user.id, ...participantIds])].sort();\n        if (allParticipantIds.length < 2) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'A chat requires at least two unique participants.' });\n        }\n        for (const pId of allParticipantIds) {\n            const participantUser = await usersDB.findById(pId);\n            if (!participantUser || !participantUser.isActive) {\n                return res.status(404).json({ message: `Participant with ID ${pId} not found or is inactive.` });\n            }\n        }\n        const allChats = await chatsDB.getAll();\n        const existingChat = allChats.find(c => \n            c.participants.length === allParticipantIds.length &&\n            c.participants.every(p => allParticipantIds.includes(p)) &&\n            c.workRequestId === workRequestId\n        );\n        if (existingChat) {\n            return res.status(200).json({ message: 'Chat with these participants (and work request, if specified) already exists.', chat: existingChat });\n        }\n        const now = new Date().toISOString();\n        const chatId = crypto.randomUUID();\n        const newChatData: Chat = {\n            id: chatId,\n            participants: allParticipantIds,\n            workRequestId: workRequestId,\n            createdAt: now,\n            updatedAt: now,\n        };\n        const createdChat = await chatsDB.create(newChatData);\n        if (initialMessageContent && typeof initialMessageContent === 'string' && initialMessageContent.trim() !== '') {\n            const messageId = crypto.randomUUID();\n            const initialMessage: Message = {\n                id: messageId,\n                chatId: createdChat.id,\n                senderId: user.id,\n                content: initialMessageContent,\n                timestamp: now,\n            };\n            await messagesDB.create(initialMessage);\n        }\n        res.status(201).json(createdChat);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error creating chat:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to create chat.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nrouter.post('/chat/:chatId/messages', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const chatId = req.params.chatId;\n        const { content, images } = req.body;\n\n        if ((!content || typeof content !== 'string' || content.trim() === '') && (!images || !Array.isArray(images) || images.length === 0)) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Message content or images are required.' });\n        }\n        if (images && (!Array.isArray(images) || !images.every(img => typeof img === 'string'))) {\n             return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Images must be an array of strings (file paths).' });\n        }\n        const chat = await chatsDB.findById(chatId);\n        if (!chat) {\n            return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Chat not found.' });\n        }\n        if (!chat.participants.includes(user.id)) {\n            return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Forbidden. You are not a participant of this chat.' });\n        }\n        const now = new Date().toISOString();\n        const messageId = crypto.randomUUID();\n        const newMessage: Message = {\n            id: messageId,\n            chatId: chatId,\n            senderId: user.id,\n            content: content || '', \n            images: images || [],\n            timestamp: now,\n        };\n        const createdMessage = await messagesDB.create(newMessage);\n        await chatsDB.update(chatId, { updatedAt: now });\n        res.status(201).json(createdMessage);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error sending message:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to send message.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nrouter.get('/chat/:chatId/messages', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const chatId = req.params.chatId;\n        const chat = await chatsDB.findById(chatId);\n        if (!chat) {\n            return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Chat not found.' });\n        }\n        if (!chat.participants.includes(user.id)) {\n            return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Forbidden. You are not a participant of this chat.' });\n        }\n        const allMessages = await messagesDB.getAll();\n        const chatMessages = allMessages\n            .filter(msg => msg.chatId === chatId)\n            .sort((a, b) => new Date(a.timestamp).getTime() - new Date(b.timestamp).getTime());\n        res.status(200).json(chatMessages);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error(`Error fetching messages for chat ${req.params.chatId}:`, error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch messages.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\n// --- Contract Endpoints ---\nrouter.get('/contracts', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const allContracts = await contractsDB.getAll();\n        const userContracts = allContracts.filter(contract => \n            contract.customerId === user.id || contract.expertSupplierId === user.id\n        );\n        userContracts.sort((a,b) => new Date(b.createdAt).getTime() - new Date(a.createdAt).getTime());\n        res.status(200).json(userContracts);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error fetching contracts:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to fetch contracts.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nrouter.post('/contracts', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const initiator = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const { workRequestId, customerId, expertSupplierId, workDetails, agreementSummary, contractDate } = req.body;\n\n        if (!workRequestId || !customerId || !expertSupplierId || !workDetails || !agreementSummary) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'workRequestId, customerId, expertSupplierId, workDetails, and agreementSummary are required.' });\n        }\n        const workRequest = await workRequestsDB.findById(workRequestId);\n        if (!workRequest) return res.status(404).json({ message: `Work request with ID ${workRequestId} not found.` });\n        if (workRequest.customerId !== customerId) {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Mismatch: workRequest.customerId does not match provided customerId.'});\n        }\n        const customer = await usersDB.findById(customerId);\n        const provider = await usersDB.findById(expertSupplierId); \n        if (!customer || !customer.isActive) return res.status(404).json({ message: `Customer with ID ${customerId} not found or inactive.` });\n        if (!provider || !provider.isActive) return res.status(404).json({ message: `Expert/Supplier with ID ${expertSupplierId} not found or inactive.` });\n        if (provider.userType !== 'expert' && provider.userType !== 'supplier') {\n            return res.status(400).json({ message: 'expertSupplierId must belong to an expert or supplier user.' });\n        }\n        if (initiator.id !== customerId && initiator.id !== expertSupplierId) {\n            return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Forbidden. Contract creator must be the customer or the designated expert/supplier.' });\n        }\n        const now = new Date().toISOString();\n        const contractId = crypto.randomUUID();\n        const newContract: Contract = {\n            id: contractId,\n            workRequestId,\n            customerId,\n            expertSupplierId,\n            workDetails,\n            agreementSummary,\n            contractDate: contractDate || now,\n            customerSigned: false,\n            expertSupplierSigned: false,\n            status: 'draft',\n            createdAt: now,\n            updatedAt: now,\n        };\n        const createdContract = await contractsDB.create(newContract);\n        if (workRequest.status === 'in_discussion' || workRequest.status === 'open' || workRequest.status === 'awaiting_quotes') {\n            await workRequestsDB.update(workRequest.id, { status: 'contracted', updatedAt: now });\n        }\n        res.status(201).json(createdContract);\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error creating contract:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        if (errorMessage.includes('already exists')) {\n            return res.status(409).json({ message: errorMessage });\n        }\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to create contract.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nrouter.put('/contracts/:contractId/sign', authenticateToken, async (req: AuthenticatedRequest, res: Response) => {\n    try {\n        const user = req.user as JwtPayload;\n        const contractId = req.params.contractId;\n        const contract = await contractsDB.findById(contractId);\n        if (!contract) {\n            return res.status(404).json({ message: 'Contract not found.' });\n        }\n        if (contract.status !== 'draft' && contract.status !== 'awaiting_signatures') {\n             return res.status(400).json({ message: `Contract in status '${contract.status}' cannot be signed. Must be 'draft' or 'awaiting_signatures'.` });\n        }\n        let signedByCurrentUser = false;\n        const now = new Date().toISOString();\n        const updates: Partial<Contract> = { updatedAt: now };\n\n        if (user.id === contract.customerId) {\n            if (contract.customerSigned) return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Customer has already signed this contract.' });\n            updates.customerSigned = true;\n            updates.customerSignatureTimestamp = now;\n            signedByCurrentUser = true;\n        } else if (user.id === contract.expertSupplierId) {\n            if (contract.expertSupplierSigned) return res.status(400).json({ message: 'Expert/Supplier has already signed this contract.' });\n            updates.expertSupplierSigned = true;\n            updates.expertSupplierSignatureTimestamp = now;\n            signedByCurrentUser = true;\n        } else {\n            return res.status(403).json({ message: 'Forbidden. You are not a party to this contract or cannot sign it.' });\n        }\n\n        if (signedByCurrentUser) {\n            const customerHasSigned = updates.customerSigned || contract.customerSigned;\n            const providerHasSigned = updates.expertSupplierSigned || contract.expertSupplierSigned;\n\n            if (customerHasSigned && providerHasSigned) {\n                updates.status = 'signed';\n            } else {\n                updates.status = 'awaiting_signatures';\n            }\n            const updatedContract = await contractsDB.update(contractId, updates);\n            res.status(200).json(updatedContract);\n        } else {\n            res.status(400).json({ message: 'No signature action was performed. User may have already signed or is not a party.' });\n        }\n    } catch (error: unknown) {\n        console.error('Error signing contract:', error);\n        const errorMessage = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unknown error occurred.';\n        res.status(500).json({ message: 'Failed to sign contract.', error: errorMessage });\n    }\n});\n\nconsole.log('API routes module (routes.ts) initialized with core endpoints.');\nexport default router;",
```

## Agent

The typo in the Google Sign-In mock response message in `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts` has been corrected.

Now, let's review and refine the backend endpoints.

## Backend Endpoint Review & Refinements

Here's a breakdown of the review for each endpoint group:

**1. File Upload (`POST /api/upload` in `server.ts`)**

- **Purpose**: Handles single file uploads.
- **Current State**:
  - Uses `multer` with disk storage, creating unique filenames in the `uploads/` directory.
  - Serves these files statically via `/uploads`.
  - Accepts a single file under the field name `file`.
  - Returns a JSON response with `filePath`, `fileName`, `originalName`, `mimeType`, and `size`.
  - Basic file size limit (10MB) is in place.
  - No specific authentication on this endpoint.
- **Review & Refinements**:
  - **Authentication**: For a demo, public upload might be acceptable. For a production app, this endpoint should be protected (e.g., require JWT) to prevent unauthorized uploads. The uploaded file's path could then be associated with the authenticated user.
  - **File Type Validation**: Currently accepts any file type. You might want to restrict this to images (e.g., `jpeg`, `png`, `gif`) using Multer's `fileFilter` option, especially since these uploads are likely for user avatars, work request images, or chat images.
    - _Suggestion_: Add a `fileFilter` to `multer` in `server.ts` to allow only common image types. This would make the upload endpoint more robust for its intended use.
  - **Error Handling**: The global error handler in `server.ts` catches `MulterError`, which is good. Specific messages for file type errors (if `fileFilter` is added) would also be good.

**2. Authentication Endpoints (`/api/auth/...` in `routes.ts`)**

- **`POST /register`**:
  - **Current State**: Validates input, checks for existing email, hashes password, creates a new user (`isActive: true`), and returns user data (sans password) and a JWT.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Solid. The profile validation using `validateProfileData` is flexible.
- **`POST /login`**:
  - **Current State**: Validates input, finds user, checks `isActive`, checks for password existence (for email/pass vs. Google-only users), compares password, returns user data (sans password) and JWT.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Good. The message "Login with email/password not available. Try Google Sign-In." is helpful for users who signed up via Google.
- **`GET /me`**:
  - **Current State**: Uses `authenticateToken` middleware. Fetches and returns the authenticated user's data (sans password). Checks `isActive`.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Correctly implemented.
- **`POST /google` (Mock Flow)**:
  - **Current State**: Simulates Google Sign-In if `mockGoogleUser` object is provided. Finds or creates a user, links Google ID, generates JWT.
  - **Review & Refinements**:
    - The mock flow is useful for development.
    - The logic for `user.userType = user.userType || userType;` means if an existing user (e.g., registered via email with type 'customer') signs in via Google mock with a different `userType` in the mock data, their original `userType` would be preserved. If the existing user _didn't_ have a `userType` (which shouldn't happen with current registration), it would be set. This seems reasonable.
    - Profile merging `user.profile = { ...user.profile, ...profileValidation.validatedProfile };` is good.

**3. Work Request Endpoints (`/api/work-requests/...` in `routes.ts`)**

- **`GET /`**:
  - **Current State**: Fetches all work requests, sorts by `createdAt` (newest first). Publicly accessible.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Good for a demo. Consider pagination and filtering for a production version.
- **`POST /`**:
  - **Current State**: Requires authentication and `userType: 'customer'`. Validates `title`, `description`, `location`. `images` must be an array of strings. `expectedCost` must be a number if provided.
  - **Review & Refinements**:
    - Authorization check is correct.
    - Validation for `images` (array of strings) is good.
    - The `expectedCost` handling (`expectedCost === undefined ? undefined : Number(expectedCost)`) and the interface `expectedCost?: number` allow it to be optional, which is good. The current code (from the last edit output) has `expectedCost: expectedCost,` which relies on the client sending a number or `undefined`. The validation `if (expectedCost !== undefined && typeof expectedCost !== 'number')` enforces that if it's present, it's a number. This is fine.
- **`GET /:id`**:
  - **Current State**: Fetches a specific work request by ID. Publicly accessible.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Standard and correct.

**4. User List Endpoints (`/api/users/...` in `routes.ts`)**

- **`GET /experts`**:
  - **Current State**: Fetches all active users with `userType: 'expert'`. Excludes passwords.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Correct.
- **`GET /suppliers`**:
  - **Current State**: Fetches all active users with `userType: 'supplier'`. Excludes passwords.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Correct.

**5. User Interest Endpoint (`POST /api/users/interest` in `routes.ts`)**

- **Current State**: Requires authentication. Takes `targetUserId`, optional `workRequestId`, and `predefinedMessageKey`. Validates users, finds/creates a chat, sends a predefined initial message, and updates the work request's interested parties.
- **Review & Refinements**:
  - This is a crucial endpoint connecting users. The logic seems well-thought-out.
  - Sorting participant IDs for chat consistency (`[sender.id, targetUserId].sort()`) is good practice for finding existing chats, even though chat IDs are UUIDs here.
  - Updating `workRequest.interestedExperts` or `interestedSuppliers` using `[...wrExperts, sender.id]` after checking for existence is correct.
  - The `predefinedMessageKey` allows for context-specific initial messages, which enhances UX.

**6. Chat Endpoints (`/api/chat/...` in `routes.ts`)**

- **`GET /`**:
  - **Current State**: Requires authentication. Fetches all chats for the authenticated user, sorted by last activity.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Correct.
- **`POST /` (Direct Chat Creation)**:
  - **Current State**: Requires authentication. Takes `participantIds`, optional `workRequestId`, and `initialMessageContent`. Adds initiator to participants, validates participants, checks for existing similar chat (returns `200 OK` with existing if found), creates chat, and optionally sends an initial message.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Good. Returning `200 OK` for an existing chat is user-friendly. Validating that all `participantIds` exist and are active is important.
- **`GET /:chatId/messages`**:
  - **Current State**: Requires authentication. User must be a participant. Fetches messages for the chat, sorted by timestamp (oldest first).
  - **Review & Refinements**: Correct.
- **`POST /:chatId/messages`**:
  - **Current State**: Requires authentication. User must be a participant. `content` or `images` required. Creates message and updates chat's `updatedAt`.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Good. Allowing message with only images (empty content string) is flexible.

**7. Contract Endpoints (`/api/contracts/...` in `routes.ts`)**

- **`GET /`**:
  - **Current State**: Requires authentication. Fetches contracts where the user is `customerId` or `expertSupplierId`, sorted by `createdAt`.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Correct.
- **`POST /`**:
  - **Current State**: Requires authentication. Validates required fields, `workRequest`, `customer`, and `provider`. Ensures initiator is one of the main parties. Creates contract with `status: 'draft'`. Optionally updates work request status to `'contracted'`.
  - **Review & Refinements**:
    - Thorough validation of involved parties and their roles.
    - Updating work request status is a good side effect.
- **`PUT /:contractId/sign`**:
  - **Current State**: Requires authentication. Validates contract exists and is in a signable status (`'draft'` or `'awaiting_signatures'`). User must be a party and not have signed yet. Updates signed status and signature timestamp. Sets contract status to `'signed'` if both parties have signed, otherwise `'awaiting_signatures'`.
  - **Review & Refinements**: Logic for signing and status transition is clear and handles the conditions correctly.

**Overall Refinements & Considerations:**

- **Error Handling**: The pattern `error: unknown` then checking `instanceof Error` is consistent. Providing `error.message` in 500 responses is useful for debugging during development.
- **Consistency**: The API generally follows RESTful principles. Status codes are used appropriately.
- **`isActive` Flag**: Consistently checked, which is good for managing user access.
- **Security (for Demo)**:
  - Passwords are correctly hashed and excluded from responses.
  - Authorization (role checks) is implemented where critical (e.g., only customers create work requests).
- **Data Integrity with `SimpleDB`**: Given it's JSON files, there are no transactional guarantees. The current approach of sequential operations is the best we can do with this storage method. For example, in `/users/interest`, if creating the message fails after creating the chat, the chat would still exist. For a demo, this is likely acceptable.
- **Idempotency**:
  - `POST /chat` is somewhat idempotent if a similar chat exists (returns 200 instead of creating a duplicate).
  - `PUT /contracts/:contractId/sign` handles cases where a user tries to sign again.
- **Scalability & Performance**: `SimpleDB` reading/writing the entire JSON file for each operation will not scale. This is understood for a demo but would be the first thing to replace with a proper database.

**Actionable Suggestion for Immediate Refinement:**

1.  **File Type Validation for Uploads**:
    - In `gefifi-2/backend/src/server.ts`, modify the `multer` setup to include a `fileFilter` to accept only common image types (e.g., `image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/gif`).
    - Update the global error handler or the `/api/upload` route to provide a specific error message if an invalid file type is uploaded.

This is the most impactful immediate refinement for robustness related to specified features (image uploads). Other points are more about future-proofing or acknowledging limitations of the demo setup.

Would you like me to implement the file type validation for uploads in `server.ts`? After that, we can consider the backend "refined" for the demo's scope, and move on to other tasks like populating sample data or starting frontend work.

## Agent

Okay, I can guide you on how to test your backend APIs using Postman and the terminal (with `curl`).

First, a quick note: you mentioned `gefifi-2/backend/package.json` changed. This was likely the `multer` version update we saw earlier. It doesn't change how you'll test the API endpoints themselves, but it's good to be aware of.

**Prerequisites:**

1.  **Start your backend server**: Navigate to `gefifi-2/backend/` in your terminal and run `bun run dev`. You should see logs indicating the server is running, typically on `http://localhost:3000`.
2.  **Postman**: Make sure you have Postman installed.
3.  **Terminal**: You'll use `curl` commands here.

**Base URL for all API requests**: `http://localhost:3000/api`
(Note: The file upload endpoint is `http://localhost:3000/api/upload`, and the root is `http://localhost:3000/`)

---

## Testing with Postman

**1. Setting up a Request:**

- **Method**: Choose GET, POST, PUT, etc., based on the endpoint.
- **URL**: Enter the full URL (e.g., `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/register`).
- **Headers**:
  - For `POST` and `PUT` requests sending JSON data, add a header:
    - `Key`: `Content-Type`
    - `Value`: `application/json`
  - For authenticated requests, you'll need an `Authorization` header (see below).
- **Body** (for `POST`, `PUT`):
  - Select the "raw" tab and choose "JSON" from the dropdown.
  - Enter your JSON payload.

**2. Handling Authentication (JWT):**

1.  **Get a Token**: First, make a request to `/auth/register` or `/auth/login`. The response will include a `token`.
2.  **Copy the Token**: Copy the value of the `token` from the response body.
3.  **Use the Token**: For subsequent authenticated requests:
    - Go to the "Authorization" tab.
    - Select "Bearer Token" from the "Type" dropdown.
    - Paste the copied token into the "Token" field on the right.
    - Alternatively, go to the "Headers" tab and add:
      - `Key`: `Authorization`
      - `Value`: `Bearer <YOUR_COPIED_TOKEN>` (replace `<YOUR_COPIED_TOKEN>` with the actual token).

**Example Postman Requests:**

- **A. Register a User**

  - **Method**: `POST`
  - **URL**: `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/register`
  - **Headers**: `Content-Type: application/json`
  - **Body** (raw, JSON):
    ```json
    {
    	"email": "customer1@example.com",
    	"password": "password123",
    	"userType": "customer",
    	"profile": {
    		"fullName": "Test Customer One",
    		"location": "Customer City"
    	}
    }
    ```
  - _Save the `token` from the response._

- **B. Login a User**

  - **Method**: `POST`
  - **URL**: `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login`
  - **Headers**: `Content-Type: application/json`
  - **Body** (raw, JSON):
    ```json
    {
    	"email": "customer1@example.com",
    	"password": "password123"
    }
    ```
  - _Save the `token` from the response._

- **C. Get Authenticated User Details (`/auth/me`)**

  - **Method**: `GET`
  - **URL**: `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/me`
  - **Authorization**: Bearer Token (paste the token from login/register)

- **D. Upload a File**

  - **Method**: `POST`
  - **URL**: `http://localhost:3000/api/upload`
  - **Body**:
    - Select the "form-data" tab.
    - In the `KEY` column, enter `file`.
    - In the `VALUE` column, click the dropdown and select "File".
    - Click "Select Files" and choose an image from your computer.
  - **Headers**: Postman usually sets `Content-Type: multipart/form-data` automatically for form-data. No `Authorization` header is currently needed for this endpoint.

- **E. Create a Work Request (Customer)**

  - **Method**: `POST`
  - **URL**: `http://localhost:3000/api/work-requests`
  - **Headers**: `Content-Type: application/json`
  - **Authorization**: Bearer Token (use token of a 'customer' user)
  - **Body** (raw, JSON):
    ```json
    {
    	"title": "Kitchen Renovation",
    	"description": "Need to renovate the entire kitchen, new cabinets and countertops.",
    	"images": ["/uploads/some-previous-upload.jpg"], // Optional: use a path from a previous upload
    	"location": "123 Main St, Anytown",
    	"expectedCost": 20000,
    	"timeline": "3 months",
    	"materialsSuggested": "Granite countertops, Shaker cabinets",
    	"category": "Renovation"
    }
    ```

- **F. Get All Work Requests**
  - **Method**: `GET`
  - **URL**: `http://localhost:3000/api/work-requests`
  - _(No specific headers or body needed, no auth currently)_

---

## Testing with `curl` (Terminal)

`curl` is powerful but can be more verbose. Replace `<YOUR_TOKEN>` with the actual JWT.

- **A. Register a User**

  ```bash
  curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "email": "expert1@example.com",
    "password": "password123",
    "userType": "expert",
    "profile": {
      "fullName": "Test Expert One",
      "expertise": "Plumbing",
      "experience": "10 years",
      "location": "Expert City"
    }
  }'
  ```

  _(You'll need to manually copy the token from the JSON response.)_

- **B. Login a User**

  ```bash
  curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "email": "expert1@example.com",
    "password": "password123"
  }'
  ```

  _(Again, copy the token from the response.)_

- **C. Get Authenticated User Details (`/auth/me`)**

  ```bash
  curl -X GET http://localhost:3000/api/auth/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_TOKEN>"
  ```

- **D. Upload a File**
  (Replace `path/to/your/image.jpg` with an actual path to a file)

  ```bash
  curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/upload \
  -F "file=@path/to/your/image.jpg"
  ```

- **E. Create a Work Request (Customer)**
  (Assuming you have a customer's token)

  ```bash
  curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/work-requests \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <CUSTOMER_TOKEN>" \
  -d '{
    "title": "Bathroom Tiling",
    "description": "Need new tiles for bathroom floor and walls.",
    "location": "456 Oak Ave, Anytown",
    "expectedCost": 5000,
    "timeline": "2 weeks"
  }'
  ```

- **F. Get All Work Requests**
  ```bash
  curl -X GET http://localhost:3000/api/work-requests
  ```

---

**General Tips for Testing:**

- **Check Server Logs**: Your backend server running in the terminal will output logs. These are invaluable for debugging if requests aren't behaving as expected.
- **Start Simple**: Test unauthenticated GET endpoints first, then registration/login, then authenticated endpoints.
- **User Roles**: Remember that some endpoints (like creating a work request) are restricted by user type. Make sure you're using a token from a user with the correct role.
- **JSON Validators**: If you're getting 400 Bad Request errors, ensure your JSON body is valid. You can use an online JSON validator.
- **Mock Google Sign-In**: For the `POST /api/auth/google` endpoint with the mock flow:
  ```json
  // Postman Body for Mock Google Sign-In
  {
  	"mockGoogleUser": {
  		"email": "googleuser@example.com",
  		"googleId": "google12345unique",
  		"name": "Google User Test",
  		"userType": "customer", // or "expert", "supplier"
  		"profile": {
  			"fullName": "Google User Test",
  			"location": "Googletown"
  		}
  	}
  }
  ```

This should give you a solid start for testing all your backend APIs! Let me know if you want examples for any other specific endpoints.

## User

what are the datas needed for registering a Customer, Expert and supplier

## Agent

You're right to ask for clarity on the registration data! Based on the backend logic we've implemented in `gefifi-2/backend/src/routes.ts` (specifically the `/api/auth/register` endpoint and the `validateProfileData` helper) and the `User` and `UserProfile` interfaces, here's what's needed for each user type:

**Common Required Fields for All User Types at Registration:**

When you make a `POST` request to `/api/auth/register`, the following fields are **mandatory** in the JSON body:

1.  `email` (string): A valid email address. This will be checked for uniqueness.
2.  `password` (string): Must be at least 6 characters long.
3.  `userType` (string): Must be one of `"customer"`, `"expert"`, or `"supplier"`.
4.  `profile` (object): An object containing profile information. While the `profile` object itself is required, its _internal fields are largely optional_ for basic registration according to the current `validateProfileData` logic. However, providing relevant profile fields is highly recommended.

**Profile Fields (inside the `profile` object):**

The `profile` object is flexible. The `validateProfileData` function currently checks the _types_ of common fields if they are provided, but it doesn't strictly enforce many of them as _required for registration itself_. For a richer demo and application, you'd populate these as appropriate.

**1. Customer Registration Profile Data:**

- **Core fields**: `email`, `password`, `userType: "customer"`
- **Recommended `profile` object fields**:

  - `fullName` (string, e.g., "Ramesh Kumar")
  - `phoneNumber` (string, e.g., "+91 9876543210")
  - `location` (string, e.g., "Koramangala, Bangalore" - could be city or full address)
  - `avatarUrl` (string, e.g., "/uploads/customer_avatar.jpg" - path to an uploaded image, optional)

  **Example JSON body for Customer registration:**

  ```json
  {
  	"email": "ramesh@example.com",
  	"password": "password123",
  	"userType": "customer",
  	"profile": {
  		"fullName": "Ramesh Kumar",
  		"phoneNumber": "+919876543210",
  		"location": "Koramangala, Bangalore"
  	}
  }
  ```

**2. Expert Registration Profile Data:**

- **Core fields**: `email`, `password`, `userType: "expert"`
- **Recommended `profile` object fields**:

  - `fullName` (string, e.g., "Ravi Mason")
  - `phoneNumber` (string, e.g., "+91 9988776655")
  - `location` (string, e.g., "Bangalore East" - their service area or base location)
  - `expertise` (string, e.g., "Mason Work", "Plumbing", "Electrical")
  - `experience` (string, e.g., "8 years", "10+ years")
  - `avatarUrl` (string, optional)
  - _(Other optional fields from `UserProfile` interface like `serviceArea`, `portfolioImages` could be added here if the frontend supports them and backend is updated to store them)._

  **Example JSON body for Expert registration:**

  ```json
  {
  	"email": "ravi.mason@example.com",
  	"password": "securepassword",
  	"userType": "expert",
  	"profile": {
  		"fullName": "Ravi Mason",
  		"phoneNumber": "+919988776655",
  		"location": "Bangalore East",
  		"expertise": "Mason Work",
  		"experience": "8 years"
  	}
  }
  ```

**3. Supplier Registration Profile Data:**

- **Core fields**: `email`, `password`, `userType: "supplier"`
- **Recommended `profile` object fields**:

  - `companyName` (string, e.g., "Kumar Building Materials") - This is a key field for suppliers.
  - `phoneNumber` (string, e.g., "+91 7766554433")
  - `location` (string, e.g., "Whitefield, Bangalore" - shop location or service area)
  - `category` (string, e.g., "Cement & Steel", "Paints & Finishes", "Sanitaryware") - The type of materials they supply.
  - `experience` (string, e.g., "15 years")
  - `avatarUrl` (string, e.g., "/uploads/shop_logo.png" - typically a company logo, optional)
  - _(Other optional fields like `serviceArea`, `businessRegistrationNumber` could be added)._

  **Example JSON body for Supplier registration:**

  ```json
  {
  	"email": "materials.shop@example.com",
  	"password": "strongpassword",
  	"userType": "supplier",
  	"profile": {
  		"companyName": "Kumar Building Materials",
  		"phoneNumber": "+917766554433",
  		"location": "Whitefield, Bangalore",
  		"category": "Cement & Steel",
  		"experience": "15 years"
  	}
  }
  ```

**Key Takeaway:**

- The backend validation for `profile` fields _during registration_ is currently somewhat lenient (it mostly checks data types if fields are provided). For a production system or a more complete demo, you might want to make some profile fields mandatory based on `userType` by updating the `validateProfileData` function or adding more checks in the registration route.
- The current structure allows you to send any of the fields defined in the `UserProfile` interface. The examples above highlight the most relevant ones for each type as per your initial spec and demo requirements.

## User

how to upload profile avatar image here. postman or terminal example

## Agent

Yes, the `backend/package.json` file changed, likely due to the `multer` version update we previously discussed. This won't alter the process for uploading avatar images.

You can upload a profile avatar image using the general file upload endpoint we've set up: `POST /api/upload`. After uploading, you'll get back a `filePath` (e.g., `/uploads/your-image-filename.jpg`). You would then typically store this `filePath` in the user's `profile.avatarUrl` field.

This means the avatar upload is a **two-step process from the client's perspective**:

1.  **Upload the image**: Send the image file to `POST /api/upload`.
2.  **Update the user's profile**: Take the `filePath` returned from the upload and make a separate request (e.g., a `PUT /api/users/me/profile` or similar endpoint, which we haven't explicitly defined yet but would be a common pattern) to update the user's `profile.avatarUrl` with this path.

For the demo, and based on the current `/api/auth/register` endpoint, you could:
a. Upload the avatar _before_ registration, get the path.
b. Include this path in the `profile.avatarUrl` field when calling `/api/auth/register`.

Or, if a user is already registered:
a. Upload a new avatar.
b. Implement an endpoint (e.g., `PUT /api/auth/me/profile`) that allows an authenticated user to update their profile, including the `avatarUrl`.

Let's focus on **Step 1: Uploading the image** using Postman and `curl`. The endpoint is `POST http://localhost:3000/api/upload`.

---

## Uploading an Avatar Image with Postman

1.  **Method**: `POST`
2.  **URL**: `http://localhost:3000/api/upload`
3.  **Body Tab**:
    - Select `form-data`.
    - In the table that appears:
      - **KEY**: Enter `file` (this is the field name your Multer setup in `server.ts` expects: `upload.single('file')`).
      - **VALUE**:
        - Click on the text input area under `VALUE`. A dropdown should appear.
        - Select "File" from this dropdown.
        - An input field and a "Select Files" button will appear. Click "Select Files".
        - Choose the avatar image you want to upload from your computer.
4.  **Headers Tab**:
    - Postman usually automatically adds the `Content-Type` header with a value like `multipart/form-data; boundary=<calculated_boundary>` when you use `form-data`. You generally don't need to add this manually.
    - **Authentication**: Currently, your `/api/upload` endpoint in `server.ts` _does not_ have the `authenticateToken` middleware.
      - If you want it to be an authenticated action (recommended for production, so only logged-in users can upload), you would add the `authenticateToken` middleware to this route in `server.ts`, and then you'd add your `Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_TOKEN>` header here in Postman.
      - For now, assuming it's unauthenticated as per the current `server.ts`.
5.  **Send the request.**

**Expected Response (Success - Status 200 OK):**

```json
{
	"message": "File uploaded successfully!",
	"filePath": "/uploads/file-1678886400000-123456789.jpg", // Example path
	"fileName": "file-1678886400000-123456789.jpg",
	"originalName": "my_avatar.jpg",
	"mimeType": "image/jpeg",
	"size": 102400 // Size in bytes
}
```

You would then take the `filePath` value (e.g., `/uploads/file-1678886400000-123456789.jpg`) and use it.

---

## Uploading an Avatar Image with `curl` (Terminal)

Replace `path/to/your/avatar_image.jpg` with the actual local path to the image file you want to upload.

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/upload \
  -F "file=@path/to/your/avatar_image.jpg"
```

**Explanation:**

- `-X POST`: Specifies the HTTP POST method.
- `http://localhost:3000/api/upload`: The URL of your upload endpoint.
- `-F "file=@path/to/your/avatar_image.jpg"`: This is how you send a file with `curl` for a `multipart/form-data` request.
  - `file`: This matches the field name expected by Multer on your server (`upload.single('file')`).
  - `@`: The `@` symbol tells `curl` that the following string is a file path, and it should read the content of that file.
  - `path/to/your/avatar_image.jpg`: The actual path to the image on your local system.

**Expected Response (Success - Status 200 OK):**
The response will be a JSON string printed to your terminal, similar to the Postman example:

```json
{
	"message": "File uploaded successfully!",
	"filePath": "/uploads/file-1678886400001-987654321.png",
	"fileName": "file-1678886400001-987654321.png",
	"originalName": "avatar.png",
	"mimeType": "image/png",
	"size": 51200
}
```

---

**How to Use the Uploaded Path for Registration:**

Let's say you upload an avatar and get back `"/uploads/avatar123.jpg"`.

When registering a user (e.g., a customer), your JSON body for `POST /api/auth/register` would look like this:

```json
{
	"email": "newuser@example.com",
	"password": "password123",
	"userType": "customer",
	"profile": {
		"fullName": "New User FullName",
		"location": "New User City",
		"avatarUrl": "/uploads/avatar123.jpg" // The path from the upload response
	}
}
```

The `avatarUrl` field in the `profile` object will then be saved in your `users.json` file for that user. Your frontend application would then use this path (e.g., `http://localhost:3000/uploads/avatar123.jpg`) to display the image.

**Important Note on Authentication for Uploads (Future Consideration):**

As mentioned, the current `/api/upload` endpoint is unauthenticated. For a real application:

1.  You'd add the `authenticateToken` middleware to the `app.post('/api/upload', ...)` route in `gefifi-2/backend/src/server.ts`.
2.  When uploading, you'd include the `Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>` header in your Postman or `curl` request.
3.  You might also want to associate the uploaded file with the authenticated user (e.g., by creating a subfolder for each user or prefixing filenames with user ID) to manage user files more effectively, but this is more advanced than the current simple setup. For the demo, having a common `uploads` folder is fine.

```svelte
					</div>

						</p>

				</div>
			</div>
		</div>
	</section>

	<!-- Footer -->
	<footer class="border-t border-slate-700/50 bg-slate-900/80 py-8 text-center">
		<div class="container mx-auto px-6">
			<p class="text-slate-400">&copy; {new Date().getFullYear()} GEFIFI. All rights reserved.</p>
			<p class="mt-1 text-sm text-slate-500">Revolutionizing Construction in India.</p>
		</div>
	</footer>
</div>

<style lang="postcss">
	/* Custom animation for hero blobs if needed */
	.animate-blob {
		animation: blob 7s infinite;
	}
	.animation-delay-2000 {
		animation-delay: 2s;
	}
	.animation-delay-4000 {
		animation-delay: 4s;
	}
	@keyframes blob {
		0% {
			transform: scale(1) translate(0, 0);
		}
		33% {
			transform: scale(1.1) translate(20px, -30px);
		}
		66% {
			transform: scale(0.9) translate(-20px, 20px);
		}
		100% {
			transform: scale(1) translate(0, 0);
		}
	}
	.animate-pulse-slow {
		animation: pulse-slow 5s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
	}

	@keyframes pulse-slow {
		0%,
		100% {
			opacity: 0.8;
			transform: scale(1);
		}
		50% {
			opacity: 1;
			transform: scale(1.02);
		}
	}
</style>
```

- A simple footer.

```diff
+  /* Custom animation for hero blobs if needed */
+  .animate-blob {
+    animation: blob 7s infinite;
+  }
+  .animation-delay-2000 {
+    animation-delay: 2s;
+  }
+  .animation-delay-4000 {
+    animation-delay: 4s;
+  }
+  @keyframes blob {
+    0% {
+      transform: scale(1) translate(0px, 0px);
+    }
+    33% {
+      transform: scale(1.1) translate(20px, -30px);
+    }
+    66% {
+      transform: scale(0.9) translate(-20px, 20px);
+    }
+    100% {
+      transform: scale(1) translate(0px, 0px);
+    }
+  }
+  .animate-pulse-slow {
+      animation: pulse-slow 5s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
+  }
+
+  @keyframes pulse-slow {
+    0%, 100% {
+        opacity: 0.8;
+        transform: scale(1.0);
+    }
+    50% {
+        opacity: 1;
+        transform: scale(1.02);
+    }
+  }
+
+  /* Ensures a base font style if not globally set */
+  :global(body) {
+    font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, "Noto Sans", sans-serif, "Apple Color Emoji", "Segoe UI Emoji", "Segoe UI Symbol", "Noto Color Emoji";
+  }
+</style>
```

## Agent

You're welcome! I'm glad you like the landing page. The change in `backend/package.json` is noted; it likely relates to the `multer` version adjustment we saw earlier and shouldn't impact our frontend work directly.

Now that we have a landing page, the next step is usually to define the main layout for the authenticated parts of the application. This layout would typically include common elements like a main navigation bar (perhaps different from the landing page's), a sidebar for navigation within the app, and a content area for the specific pages.

SvelteKit uses `+layout.svelte` files for this. We already have a root layout at `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte`. This root layout applies to _all_ pages.
For the authenticated section (dashboard, chat, contracts, etc.), we can create a "route group". This allows us to have a specific layout for a set of routes without changing their URLs. A common convention is to name such a group `(app)`.

So, the plan is:

1.  Review the existing root layout (`gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte`). We want to ensure it's minimal and doesn't interfere with more specific layouts. Often, it just contains a `<slot />` and perhaps global styles or scripts.
2.  Create a new layout file for the authenticated app section: `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte`.
3.  This `(app)/+layout.svelte` will contain:
    - A main header bar (e.g., with app logo, user profile dropdown).
    - A sidebar navigation menu (links to Dashboard, Chat, Contracts, Create Work Request, etc., depending on user type).
    - The main content area (`<slot />`) where individual page content will be injected.
4.  Later, we'll move routes like `/dashboard`, `/chat`, etc., into directories under this `(app)` group (e.g., `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/(app)/dashboard/+page.svelte`).

Let's first check the content of the existing root layout file: `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte`.

```json
	"path": "gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte"
}
```
