    - The `handle` function will be asynchronous.
    - It will get the current `route.id` from the `event` object.
    - It will get the `isLoggedIn` and `isLoading` status from `authStore`.
    - **Initial Load Consideration**: Since `authStore.loadUserFromStorage()` runs when the store module is imported, we need to ensure that the hook doesn't act before this initial loading is complete. We can check `authState.isLoading`. If it's true, we should ideally wait or let the navigation proceed and rely on components to handle the loading state. A pragmatic approach for a client hook is to proceed if still loading, as pages themselves should handle loading states from the store. The redirect logic should only fire if `!isLoading`.
    - **Protect App Routes**: If `route.id` starts with `/(app)/` (e.g., `/(app)/dashboard`) and `!isLoggedIn` and `!isLoading`, redirect to `/auth/login`.
    - **Redirect from Auth Routes**: If `route.id` is `/auth/login` or `/auth/register` and `isLoggedIn` and `!isLoading`, redirect to `/dashboard`.
    - **Prevent Redirect Loops**: Ensure that if the current path is already the target of a redirect, the redirect doesn't happen (e.g., if on `/auth/login` and not logged in, don't redirect to `/auth/login` again). `goto` itself usually handles this, but an explicit check on `event.url.pathname` can be added.
4.  **`handleError` Hook (Client-side)**:
    - A basic implementation to log client-side errors that occur during navigation or rendering.

Here's the code for `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/hooks.client.ts`:

```typescript
import { authStore } from '$lib/stores/auth';
import { goto } from '$app/navigation';
import { get } from 'svelte/store';
import type { Handle, HandleClientError, NavigationEvent } from '@sveltejs/kit';

export const handle: Handle = async ({ event, resolve }) => {
	const { route, url } = event;
	let authState = get(authStore); // Get current state

	// If the auth store is still loading its initial state,
	// it might be too early to redirect. We wait for one tick or a short moment.
	// The loadUserFromStorage is called on authStore import.
	if (authState.isLoading) {
		// Wait for the store to potentially update after initial load.
		// This is a simple way; a more robust system might involve the store emitting an 'initialized' event.
		await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 0)); // Wait for next tick
		authState = get(authStore); // Re-check state
	}

	const isLoggedIn = authState.isLoggedIn;
	const storeIsLoading = authState.isLoading; // Final check on loading status

	// Debug log (optional)
	// console.log(`[Hook] Path: ${url.pathname}, RouteID: ${route.id}, LoggedIn: ${isLoggedIn}, StoreLoading: ${storeIsLoading}`);

	// Protected app routes: routes starting with '/(app)'
	if (route.id?.startsWith('/(app)')) {
		if (!isLoggedIn && !storeIsLoading) {
			// console.log(`[Hook] Accessing app route ${url.pathname} while not logged in. Redirecting to /auth/login.`);
			if (url.pathname !== '/auth/login') {
				// Prevent loop if somehow /auth/login is in (app)
				throw await goto('/auth/login', { replaceState: true });
			}
		}
	}

	// Auth pages (login, register)
	if (route.id === '/auth/login' || route.id === '/auth/register') {
		if (isLoggedIn && !storeIsLoading) {
			// console.log(`[Hook] Accessing auth route ${url.pathname} while logged in. Redirecting to /dashboard.`);
			if (url.pathname !== '/dashboard') {
				// Prevent loop if /dashboard somehow fails
				throw await goto('/dashboard', { replaceState: true });
			}
		}
	}

	// Fallback: if user is not logged in and tries to access a page within (app) that isn't caught by route.id check
	// (e.g. if a dynamic route under (app) doesn't have route.id starting with '/(app)')
	// This is a broader check based on URL path.
	if (
		url.pathname.startsWith('/dashboard') ||
		url.pathname.startsWith('/chat') ||
		url.pathname.startsWith('/contracts') ||
		url.pathname.startsWith('/customer/create-request')
	) {
		if (!isLoggedIn && !storeIsLoading && !url.pathname.startsWith('/auth/')) {
			// console.log(`[Hook] Fallback: Accessing ${url.pathname} while not logged in. Redirecting.`);
			throw await goto('/auth/login', { replaceState: true });
		}
	}

	return resolve(event);
};

export const handleError: HandleClientError = ({ error, event }) => {
	console.error('[ClientHook handleError] Error:', error);
	console.error('[ClientHook handleError] Event:', event);

	// Example: You could log this to an external service
	// Sentry.captureException(error, { extra: { event } });

	return {
		message: 'An unexpected client-side error occurred. Please try again.'
		// You can add more properties if your error page expects them
	};
};
```

Redirecting to /dashboard.`);\n       if (url.pathname !== '/dashboard') { // Prevent loop if /dashboard somehow fails\n         throw await goto('/dashboard', { replaceState: true });\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fallback: if user is not logged in and tries to access a page within (app) that isn't caught by route.id check\n  // (e.g. if a dynamic route under (app) doesn't have route.id starting with '/(app)')\n  // This is a broader check based on URL path.\n  if (url.pathname.startsWith('/dashboard') || url.pathname.startsWith('/chat') || url.pathname.startsWith('/contracts') || url.pathname.startsWith('/customer/create-request')) {\n      if (!isLoggedIn && !storeIsLoading && !url.pathname.startsWith('/auth/')) {\n           // console.log(`[Hook] Fallback: Accessing ${url.pathname} while not logged in. Redirecting.`);\n           throw await goto('/auth/login', { replaceState: true });\n      }\n  }\n\n\n  return resolve(event);\n};\n\nexport const handleError: HandleClientError = ({ error, event }) => {\n  console.error('[ClientHook handleError] Error:', error);\n  console.error('[ClientHook handleError] Event:', event);\n\n  // Example: You could log this to an external service\n  // Sentry.captureException(error, { extra: { event } });\n\n  return {\n    message: 'An unexpected client-side error occurred. Please try again.',\n    // You can add more properties if your error page expects them\n  };\n};\n",
	"path": "gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/hooks.client.ts"
}

```diff
@@ -1,4 +1,146 @@
 import { authStore } from '$lib/stores/auth';
 import { goto } from '$app/navigation';
 import { get } from 'svelte/store';
-import type { Handle } from '@sveltejs/kit
+import type { HandleClientError, NavigationEvent } from '@sveltejs/kit';
+
+/**
+ * Client-side error handler for SvelteKit.
+ * This function is called when an error occurs during client-side navigation or rendering.
+ * @type {import('@sveltejs/kit').HandleClientError}
+ */
+export const handleError: HandleClientError = ({ error, event }) => {
+  console.error('[GEFIFI Client Error Hook] An error occurred:', {
+    error,
+    routeId: event.route.id,
+    url: event.url.pathname,
+  });
+
+  // You can customize the error object returned to your $error.svelte page
+  const status = (error as any)?.status || 500;
+  let message = 'An unexpected error occurred. Please try refreshing the page.';
+
+  if (error instanceof Error) {
+    // For specific known errors, you might want custom messages
+    // For example, if it's an ApiError from your API client
+    if ((error as any).name === 'ApiError') {
+      message = (error as any).data?.message || error.message || 'An API error occurred.';
+    } else {
+      message = error.message;
+    }
+  } else if (typeof error === 'string') {
+    message = error;
+  }
+
+  // Example: Send to an error tracking service
+  // Sentry.captureException(error, { extra: { event } });
+
+  return {
+    message: `Oops! ${message}`,
+    status,
+    errorId: 'CLIENT_HOOK_ERROR',
+  };
+};
+
+/*
+ * --- Client-Side Route Protection & Auth Redirects ---
+ *
+ * IMPORTANT SVELTEKIT NOTE:
+ * Client-side route protection and redirects based on authentication state are
+ * NOT typically handled in `hooks.client.ts` using a general `handle` function
+ * for navigation events (unlike `hooks.server.ts`).
+ * The `handle` export in `hooks.client.ts` is specifically for `handleFetch`,
+ * which intercepts `fetch` requests made from the client-side SvelteKit code.
+ *
+ * Instead, client-side navigation protection logic should be placed in:
+ *
+ * 1. Root Layout (`src/routes/+layout.svelte` or `src/routes/+layout.ts`):
+ *    - For client-side navigation checks after initial load:
+ *      In `+layout.svelte`, use `onMount` to subscribe to the `authStore`.
+ *      Inside the subscription, check `currentAuth.isLoggedIn` and `currentAuth.isLoading`.
+ *      If `!currentAuth.isLoading`, then you can perform redirects using `goto()`.
+ *      Alternatively, use SvelteKit's `beforeNavigate` function from `$app/navigation`
+ *      within `+layout.svelte` for more granular control over navigation attempts.
+ *
+ * 2. `load` Functions (`+layout.ts` or `+page.ts`):
+ *    - For protection during initial page load (both server-side and client-side hydration)
+ *      and subsequent client-side navigations.
+ *    - In a `load` function, you can check authentication status (e.g., from `authStore`
+ *      if running on the client, or from session/cookies if on the server via `event.locals`).
+ *    - Use `redirect()` from `@sveltejs/kit` to perform redirects within `load` functions.
+ *
+ *
+ * --- EXAMPLE LOGIC (Place in appropriate files like `src/routes/+layout.svelte`) ---
+ *
+ * This demonstrates how you might implement the checks.
+ * This is NOT for `hooks.client.ts` but for a layout file.
+ */
+
+/*
+// In a root +layout.svelte or (app)/+layout.svelte:
+
+<script lang="ts">
+  import { onMount } from 'svelte';
+  import { browser } from '$app/environment';
+  import { page } from '$app/stores'; // To get current path/route information
+  import { goto } from '$app/navigation';
+  import { authStore } from '$lib/stores/auth';
+
+  onMount(() => {
+    if (browser) {
+      const unsubscribe = authStore.subscribe(currentAuth => {
+        // Wait for the auth store to finish its initial loading
+        if (currentAuth.isLoading) {
+          // console.log('[Layout] Auth store is loading, waiting for definitive state...');
+          return;
+        }
+
+        const currentPath = $page.url.pathname;
+        const currentRouteId = $page.route.id;
+
+        // Determine if the current route is an "app" route (needs protection)
+        // This assumes your protected routes are under a group like (app)
+        // or you have another way to identify them (e.g., from $page.data)
+        const isAppRoute = currentRouteId?.includes('(app)'); // Adjust based on your route grouping
+
+        // Auth pages that logged-in users should be redirected away from
+        const isAuthRoute = currentRouteId === '/auth/login' || currentRouteId === '/auth/register';
+
+        // console.log(`[Layout Check] Path: ${currentPath}, RouteID: ${currentRouteId}, LoggedIn: ${currentAuth.isLoggedIn}`);
+
+        if (isAppRoute && !currentAuth.isLoggedIn) {
+          // console.log(`[Layout] User not logged in, trying to access ${currentPath}. Redirecting to /auth/login.`);
+          if (currentPath !== '/auth/login') { // Prevent loop if login is somehow in (app)
+            goto('/auth/login', { replaceState: true });
+          }
+        }
+
+        if (isAuthRoute && currentAuth.isLoggedIn) {
+          // console.log(`[Layout] User logged in, trying to access ${currentPath}. Redirecting to /dashboard.`);
+          if (currentPath !== '/dashboard') { // Prevent loop if dashboard is somehow an auth route
+            goto('/dashboard', { replaceState: true });
+          }
+        }
+      });
+
+      // Call loadUserFromStorage if it's not automatically called on store import
+      // and needs to be triggered by the layout. (It's currently called on import in auth.ts)
+      // authStore.loadUserFromStorage();
+
+      return unsubscribe; // Cleanup subscription on component destroy
+    }
+  });
+</script>
+
+<slot />
+
+*/
+
+// If you need to intercept fetch requests globally (e.g., to add headers, not for navigation):
+// export const handleFetch: HandleFetch = async ({ request, fetch }) => {
+//   console.log('[Client Hook] handleFetch intercepting:', request.url);
+//   // Example: Add a custom header to all outgoing fetch requests from the client
+//   // if (request.url.startsWith(API_BASE_URL)) { // Assuming API_BASE_URL is defined
+//   //   request.headers.set('X-Custom-Client-Header', 'gefifi-app');
+//   // }
+//   return fetch(request);
+// };

// To get current path/route information\n  import { goto } from '$app/navigation';\n  import { authStore } from '$lib/stores/auth';\n\n  onMount(() => {\n    if (browser) {\n      const unsubscribe = authStore.subscribe(currentAuth => {\n        // Wait for the auth store to finish its initial loading\n        if (currentAuth.isLoading) {\n          // console.log('[Layout] Auth store is loading, waiting for definitive state...');\n          return;\n        }\n\n        const currentPath = $page.url.pathname;\n        const currentRouteId = $page.route.id;\n\n        // Determine if the current route is an \"app\" route (needs protection)\n        // This assumes your protected routes are under a group like (app)\n        // or you have another way to identify them (e.g., from $page.data)\n        const isAppRoute = currentRouteId?.includes('(app)'); // Adjust based on your route grouping\n\n        // Auth pages that logged-in users should be redirected away from\n        const isAuthRoute = currentRouteId === '/auth/login' || currentRouteId === '/auth/register';\n\n        // console.log(`[Layout Check] Path: ${currentPath}, RouteID: ${currentRouteId}, LoggedIn: ${currentAuth.isLoggedIn}`);\n\n        if (isAppRoute && !currentAuth.isLoggedIn) {\n          // console.log(`[Layout] User not logged in, trying to access ${currentPath}. Redirecting to /auth/login.`);\n          if (currentPath !== '/auth/login') { // Prevent loop if login is somehow in (app)\n            goto('/auth/login', { replaceState: true });\n          }\n        }\n\n        if (isAuthRoute && currentAuth.isLoggedIn) {\n          // console.log(`[Layout] User logged in, trying to access ${currentPath}. Redirecting to /dashboard.`);\n          if (currentPath !== '/dashboard') { // Prevent loop if dashboard is somehow an auth route\n            goto('/dashboard', { replaceState: true });\n          }\n        }\n      });\n\n      // Call loadUserFromStorage if it's not automatically called on store import\n      // and needs to be triggered by the layout. (It's currently called on import in auth.ts)\n      // authStore.loadUserFromStorage(); \n      \n      return unsubscribe; // Cleanup subscription on component destroy\n    }\n  });\n</script>\n\n<slot />\n\n*/\n\n// If you need to intercept fetch requests globally (e.g., to add headers, not for navigation):\n// export const handleFetch: HandleFetch = async ({ request, fetch }) => {\n//   console.log('[Client Hook] handleFetch intercepting:', request.url);\n//   // Example: Add a custom header to all outgoing fetch requests from the client\n//   // if (request.url.startsWith(API_BASE_URL)) { // Assuming API_BASE_URL is defined\n//   //   request.headers.set('X-Custom-Client-Header', 'gefifi-app');\n//   // }\n//   return fetch(request);\n// };\n```",

## Agent

You are correct to point out that `hooks.client.ts` with a `handle` function for navigation is not the standard SvelteKit approach. My apologies for that misdirection in the previous step. The `handle` export in `hooks.client.ts` is primarily for intercepting `fetch` requests (`handleFetch`) and the `handleError` function.

Client-side navigation protection and redirection based on authentication state are best handled within layout files (e.g., `+layout.svelte` or associated `+layout.ts`) or using SvelteKit's `load` functions which can issue redirects.

Given that our `authStore` is a client-side Svelte store that initializes from `localStorage`, we should implement the redirection logic in a place that runs on the client and can react to store changes. The root layout (`gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte`) is a good place for this, as it can control access to all routes.

I acknowledge the change in `backend/package.json`; this is likely the `multer` version update we've discussed and does not affect this frontend task.

**Revised Plan: Implement Client-Side Route Protection in Root Layout**

We will modify `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte` to include:

1.  Subscription to `authStore`.
2.  Logic within `onMount` (to ensure it runs on the client) that checks the authentication state and current path.
3.  Redirection using `goto` from `$app/navigation` if:
    - An unauthenticated user tries to access a protected route (e.g., `/dashboard`, `/chat`, etc., which are effectively any route that would fall under our `(app)` layout).
    - An authenticated user tries to access `/auth/login` or `/auth/register`.

Let's get the current content of the root layout file `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte`.

}

## Agent

Okay, I understand the previous approach with `hooks.client.ts` for navigation redirection was not the SvelteKit standard. We'll implement the client-side route protection logic directly in the root layout file (`gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte`).

This layout will subscribe to the `authStore` and perform redirects based on the user's authentication status and the current route.

I acknowledge the change in `backend/package.json`; this is likely the `multer` version update and doesn't affect this frontend task.

Here's the updated content for `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte`:

```svelte
<!-- gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte -->
<script lang="ts">
	import '../app.css'; // Import global styles, including Tailwind base
	import { onMount } from 'svelte';
	import { browser } from '$app/environment';
	import { page } from '$app/stores'; // To get current path information
	import { goto } from '$app/navigation';
	import { authStore } from '$lib/stores/auth';

	let { children } = $props();

	onMount(() => {
		if (browser) {
			// The authStore automatically calls loadUserFromStorage on import if in browser.
			// We subscribe to changes in the auth state to react to login/logout or initial load completion.
			const unsubscribe = authStore.subscribe((currentAuth) => {
				// Wait for the auth store to finish its initial loading attempt
				if (currentAuth.isLoading) {
					// console.log('[RootLayout] Auth store is loading, waiting...');
					return;
				}

				const currentPath = $page.url.pathname;
				const isLoggedIn = currentAuth.isLoggedIn;

				// Define routes that require authentication
				const protectedAppRoutes = [
					'/dashboard',
					'/chat',
					'/contracts',
					'/customer/create-request'
					// Add any other routes that should be protected
				];

				// Define authentication pages (login, register)
				const authPages = ['/auth/login', '/auth/register'];

				// Check if currentPath starts with any of the protectedAppRoutes
				const isAccessingProtectedAppRoute = protectedAppRoutes.some((route) =>
					currentPath.startsWith(route)
				);

				// Debug log (optional)
				// console.log(`[RootLayout] Path: ${currentPath}, LoggedIn: ${isLoggedIn}, IsAppRoute: ${isAccessingProtectedAppRoute}`);

				if (isAccessingProtectedAppRoute && !isLoggedIn) {
					// console.log(`[RootLayout] User not logged in, trying to access protected route ${currentPath}. Redirecting to /auth/login.`);
					if (currentPath !== '/auth/login') {
						// Prevent loop if somehow login page is misconfigured as protected
						goto('/auth/login', { replaceState: true });
					}
				}

				if (authPages.includes(currentPath) && isLoggedIn) {
					// console.log(`[RootLayout] User logged in, trying to access auth page ${currentPath}. Redirecting to /dashboard.`);
					if (currentPath !== '/dashboard') {
						// Prevent loop
						goto('/dashboard', { replaceState: true });
					}
				}
			});

			// Cleanup subscription when the layout component is destroyed
			return () => {
				unsubscribe();
			};
		}
	});
</script>

{@render children()}

<!-- 
  You can add global elements here that should appear on ALL pages, 
  outside of specific layouts like (app)/+layout.svelte.
  For example, a global notification system, or a cookie banner.
  For this project, most of the shared UI for authenticated users is in (app)/+layout.svelte.
-->
```

**Explanation of changes:**

1.  **Imports**: Added `onMount`, `browser`, `page`, `goto`, and `authStore`.
2.  **`onMount` Block**:
    - Ensures the logic runs only on the client-side using `if (browser)`.
    - Subscribes to `authStore`. The `authStore.loadUserFromStorage()` is already called when the store module is first imported, so the store will eventually update with `isLoading: false`.
    - **Redirect Logic**:
      - It checks if `currentAuth.isLoading`. If it is, no action is taken, allowing the store to initialize.
      - It defines `protectedAppRoutes` (routes like `/dashboard`, `/chat`, etc.).
      - If the user tries to access a protected route and is not logged in (and the store is not loading), they are redirected to `/auth/login`.
      - If the user is logged in (and the store is not loading) and tries to access `/auth/login` or `/auth/register`, they are redirected to `/dashboard`.
    - **Unsubscribe**: The subscription is cleaned up when the layout is destroyed.

This setup provides client-side route protection. A full-stack application would also benefit from server-side checks (e.g., in `hooks.server.ts` or `load` functions) to prevent data leaks or unauthorized actions even if the client-side JavaScript is bypassed, but for a demo MVP, strong client-side guards are a good start.

I'll apply these changes to `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte`.

Redirecting to /auth/login.`);\n          if (currentPath !== '/auth/login') { // Prevent loop if somehow login page is misconfigured as protected\n            goto('/auth/login', { replaceState: true });\n          }\n        }\n\n        if (authPages.includes(currentPath) && isLoggedIn) {\n          // console.log(`[RootLayout] User logged in, trying to access auth page ${currentPath}. Redirecting to /dashboard.`);\n          if (currentPath !== '/dashboard') { // Prevent loop\n            goto('/dashboard', { replaceState: true });\n          }\n        }\n      });\n\n      // Cleanup subscription when the layout component is destroyed\n      return () => {\n        unsubscribe();\n      };\n    }\n  });\n</script>\n\n{@render children()}\n\n<!-- \n  You can add global elements here that should appear on ALL pages, \n  outside of specific layouts like (app)/+layout.svelte.\n  For example, a global notification system, or a cookie banner.\n  For this project, most of the shared UI for authenticated users is in (app)/+layout.svelte.\n-->\n",
	"path": "gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte"
}

## Agent

The root layout `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/+layout.svelte` has been updated with client-side route protection logic. This logic, combined with the `authStore`, `apiClient`, login, and registration pages, forms the core of your frontend authentication system.

I acknowledge the change in `backend/package.json`; this is likely the `multer` version update we've been seeing and should not affect the frontend functionality we're about to test.

**Frontend Authentication Flow Summary:**

1.  **Auth Store (`$lib/stores/auth.ts`)**: Manages `isLoggedIn`, `user`, `token`, `isLoading`, and `error` states. It interacts with `localStorage` and uses `apiClient` for backend communication (login/register). It attempts to load user state from storage on app initialization.
2.  **API Client (`$lib/api.ts`)**: Handles all HTTP requests to your backend, automatically attaching the auth token.
3.  **Login Page (`/auth/login/+page.svelte`)**: Allows users to log in using email/password or a (mocked) Google Sign-In. Uses `authStore.login()` or `apiClient.googleLogin()` and then `authStore._updateAuthData()`. Redirects to `/dashboard` on success.
4.  **Registration Page (`/auth/register/+page.svelte`)**: A multi-step form for user registration (account and profile details). Uses `authStore.register()` or `apiClient.googleLogin()` for mock Google registration. Redirects to `/dashboard` on success.
5.  **App Layout (`/(app)/+layout.svelte`)**: Subscribes to `authStore` to display user information, conditional navigation links, and logout functionality. It also has skeleton loaders and a basic "Access Denied" message if no user is logged in (though the root layout's redirect should ideally prevent this state from being shown for long).
6.  **Root Layout (`/+layout.svelte`)**: Contains the global client-side route protection logic. It subscribes to `authStore` and uses `goto` to:
    - Redirect unauthenticated users from protected app routes (like `/dashboard`, `/chat`) to `/auth/login`.
    - Redirect authenticated users from auth pages (`/auth/login`, `/auth/register`) to `/dashboard`.

**Next Step: Test the Authentication Flow**

It's now time to test this entire flow to ensure it's working as expected.

**Testing Checklist:**

1.  **Run Servers**:
    - Start your backend server: `cd gefifi-2/backend && bun run dev`
    - Start your SvelteKit frontend dev server: `cd gefifi-2/frontend && bun run dev` (or from `gefifi-2/` if your root `package.json` script handles this, e.g., `bun run dev` which might run both concurrently).
2.  **Open Frontend**: Navigate to your SvelteKit app in the browser (usually `http://localhost:5173` or a similar port).
3.  **Initial Redirect**: You should be on the landing page (`/`). Try navigating directly to `/dashboard`. The route protection in `/+layout.svelte` should redirect you to `/auth/login`.
4.  **Registration**:
    - Go to `/auth/register`.
    - Fill out the registration form (both steps) for a new user (e.g., a 'customer').
    - Submit the form.
    - **Expected**: Successful registration, and you should be redirected to `/dashboard`. The `(app)/+layout.svelte` should display (mocked or basic) user information.
5.  **Verify App Layout**: On the `/dashboard`, check if the sidebar and user info (from the mock/authStore) in the `(app)/+layout.svelte` are displaying correctly.
6.  **Logout**:
    - Click the "Logout" button in the sidebar.
    - **Expected**: You should be redirected to `/auth/login`. The `authStore` should be cleared.
7.  **Attempt Protected Route Access (Logged Out)**:
    - Try to navigate directly to `/dashboard` again.
    - **Expected**: Redirected back to `/auth/login`.
8.  **Login**:
    - On the `/auth/login` page, enter the credentials of the user you just registered.
    - Click "Login".
    - **Expected**: Successful login, redirected to `/dashboard`. User information in the layout should be correct.
9.  **Attempt Auth Page Access (Logged In)**:
    - While logged in, try to navigate directly to `/auth/login` or `/auth/register`.
    - **Expected**: Redirected back to `/dashboard`.
10. **Test Google Sign-In/Register (Mock Flow)**:
    - Log out if you are logged in.
    - Try the "Login with Google" button on the login page.

## Agent

Thank you for the detailed testing and the screenshot! This is very helpful.

It seems the main issue is the `each_key_duplicate` error related to `undefined` keys, which is preventing the sidebar (and thus the logout button and proper navigation links) from rendering correctly in the `(app)/+layout.svelte` file, especially after registration and on page refresh.

The error "Keyed each block has duplicate key `undefined`" typically happens when you're using an `{#each ... as item (key)}` block and the `key` you're providing is not unique for all items, or multiple items end up with an `undefined` key.

Let's look at the `(app)/+layout.svelte` where the `filteredNavLinks` are rendered. The `{#each filteredNavLinks as link}` doesn't explicitly use a key, so Svelte might be defaulting to the object instance or index. However, the error points to `undefined` keys.

The most likely place this `undefined` key issue is arising is in how `filteredNavLinks` is being generated, or how the `icons` are being accessed if the `link.iconKey` might somehow be undefined for some links under certain conditions (though this seems less probable for the key itself).

Let's examine the `filteredNavLinks` logic in `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte`:

```svelte
// ... $: filteredNavLinks = navLinks.filter(link => link.types.includes('all') ||
(currentAuth.user?.userType && link.types.includes(currentAuth.user.userType)) ); // ...
<nav class="flex-grow space-y-2 overflow-y-auto p-4">
	{#if currentAuth.isLoading && !currentAuth.isLoggedIn}
		<!-- Skeleton Loader for Nav -->
		{#each Array(4) as _}
			<!-- This #each is unkeyed, usually fine for static number of elements -->
			<div class="mb-2 h-10 animate-pulse rounded-lg bg-slate-700/50"></div>
		{/each}
	{:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn && currentAuth.user}
		{#each filteredNavLinks as link (link.href)}
			<!-- Let's add an explicit key -->
			<a
				href={link.href}
				class="group flex items-center space-x-3 rounded-lg px-3 py-2.5 transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out
                   {$page.url.pathname.startsWith(link.href) &&
				(link.href !== '/dashboard' || $page.url.pathname === '/dashboard')
					? 'bg-emerald-600 text-white shadow-md'
					: 'text-slate-300 hover:bg-slate-700/50 hover:text-emerald-300'}"
				on:click={() => (sidebarOpen = false)}
				title={link.label}
			>
				{@html icons[link.iconKey]}
				<span class="truncate">{link.label}</span>
			</a>
		{/each}
	{/if}
</nav>
// ...
```

**Hypothesis for the error:**

1.  **Timing Issue with `currentAuth.user`**: When the component initializes or after registration, `currentAuth.user` (and thus `currentAuth.user.userType`) might be temporarily `null` or `undefined` even if `isLoggedIn` is true, or while `isLoading` is true.
2.  **Keying in `{#each filteredNavLinks}`**: While `link.href` should be unique, if `filteredNavLinks` itself becomes problematic (e.g., containing undefined entries or if the filter logic has an edge case), it could lead to issues. Svelte requires a unique key for each item in a keyed `{#each}` block to efficiently update the DOM. If multiple links somehow ended up with an `undefined` href (which is unlikely given the static `navLinks` definition) or if Svelte's internal keying got confused due to rapid state changes, this could occur.

The error trace pointing to line 189 (`consequent +layout.svelte:189`) would be inside the `{#if currentAuth.isLoggedIn && currentAuth.user}` block, specifically around the `{#each filteredNavLinks as link}`.

**Proposed Fixes:**

**Fix 1: Ensure `link.href` is always used as a key and is defined.**
The `navLinks` array has static `href` values, which should be unique. Adding `(link.href)` as the key to the `{#each}` block is good practice.

**Fix 2: Ensure `iconKey` is valid before accessing `icons[link.iconKey]`**.
While `link.iconKey` is typed, a defensive check can prevent errors if `link.iconKey` was somehow not in `icons`.

```svelte
{#if icons[link.iconKey]}
	{@html icons[link.iconKey]}
{:else}
	<!-- Fallback icon or empty span -->
	<span class="inline-block h-5 w-5"></span>
{/if}
```

**Fix 3: Strengthen the conditions for rendering the navigation and user profile.**
The flash of a white screen after registration suggests that `currentAuth.isLoggedIn` might become true _before_ `currentAuth.user` is fully populated, or before `isLoading` becomes definitively `false`.
The `authStore.loadUserFromStorage` function now directly fetches `/api/auth/me`. If this call is pending after registration, `currentAuth.user` might be null initially.

Let's refine the conditional rendering in the layout:

- Only render the navigation links if `currentAuth.isLoggedIn` is true AND `currentAuth.user` is truthy AND `!currentAuth.isLoading`.
- The skeleton loader for nav should appear if `currentAuth.isLoading` is true.

**Revised code section for `(app)/+layout.svelte`:**

I'll focus on the `<nav>` and "User Profile / Logout" sections.
I'll read the file again to apply the changes to the correct version.

<script lang="ts">
	import { page } from '$app/stores';
	import { goto } from '$app/navigation';
	import { authStore, type AuthUser, type AuthState } from '$lib/stores/auth';

	$: currentAuth = $authStore;

	interface NavLink {
		href: string;
		label: string;
		iconKey: keyof typeof icons;
		types: Array<AuthUser['userType'] | 'all'>;
	}

	const navLinks: NavLink[] = [
		{ href: '/dashboard', label: 'Dashboard', iconKey: 'dashboard', types: ['all'] },
		{ href: '/chat', label: 'Chat', iconKey: 'chat', types: ['all'] },
		{ href: '/contracts', label: 'Contracts', iconKey: 'contracts', types: ['all'] },
		{
			href: '/customer/create-request',
			label: 'New Request',
			iconKey: 'newRequest',
			types: ['customer']
		}
	];

	async function handleLogout() {
		await authStore.logout();
		goto('/auth/login', { replaceState: true });
	}

	let sidebarOpen = false;

	// Corrected SVG quotes: replaced \\" with '
	const icons = {
		dashboard: `<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' viewBox='0 0 24 24' stroke-width='1.5' stroke='currentColor' class='w-5 h-5'><path stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round' d='M3.75 6A2.25 2.25 0 016 3.75h2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0110.5 6v2.25a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25 2.25H6a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25-2.25V6zM3.75 15.75A2.25 2.25 0 016 13.5h2.25a2.25 2.25 0 012.25 2.25V18a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25 2.25H6a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25-2.25v-2.25zM13.5 6a2.25 2.25 0 012.25-2.25H18A2.25 2.25 0 0120.25 6v2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0118 10.5h-2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0113.5 8.25V6zM13.5 15.75a2.25 2.25 0 012.25-2.25H18a2.25 2.25 0 012.25 2.25V18A2.25 2.25 0 0118 20.25h-2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0113.5 18v-2.25z' /></svg>`,
		chat: `<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' viewBox='0 0 24 24' stroke-width='1.5' stroke='currentColor' class='w-5 h-5'><path stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round' d='M20.25 8.511c.884.284 1.5 1.128 1.5 2.097v4.286c0 1.136-.847 2.1-1.98 2.193-.34.027-.68.052-1.02.072v3.091l-3.697-3.697c-.024-.024-.047-.048-.072-.071a11.91 11.91 0 01-6.185-1.347M16.5 9.75L9 14.25m7.5-4.5l-7.5 4.5M3.75 8.511c-.884.284-1.5 1.128-1.5 2.097v4.286c0 1.136.847 2.1 1.98 2.193.34.027-.68.052 1.02.072v3.091l3.697-3.697c.024-.024-.047-.048-.072-.071a11.91 11.91 0 016.185-1.347m0-2.489c.331 0 .658.025.973.069a11.909 11.909 0 00-9.054-3.996c-1.756 0-3.404.44-4.84 1.206a11.892 11.892 0 00-6.184 3.99V10.5a2.25 2.25 0 012.25-2.25h13.5A2.25 2.25 0 0020.25 6v1.5a2.25 2.25 0 00-2.25 2.25H15m-1.5-1.5l-7.5 4.5' /></svg>`,
		contracts: `<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' viewBox='0 0 24 24' stroke-width='1.5' stroke='currentColor' class='w-5 h-5'><path stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round' d='M19.5 14.25v-2.625a3.375 3.375 0 00-3.375-3.375h-1.5A1.125 1.125 0 0113.5 7.125v-1.5a3.375 3.375 0 00-3.375-3.375H8.25m0 12.75h7.5m-7.5 3H12M10.5 2.25H5.625c-.621 0-1.125.504-1.125 1.125v17.25c0 .621.504 1.125 1.125 1.125h12.75c.621 0 1.125-.504 1.125-1.125V11.25a9 9 0 00-9-9z' /></svg>`,
		newRequest: `<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' viewBox='0 0 24 24' stroke-width='1.5' stroke='currentColor' class='w-5 h-5'><path stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round' d='M12 9v6m3-3H9m12 0a9 9 0 11-18 0 9 9 0 0118 0z' /></svg>`,

		close: `<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' viewBox='0 0 24 24' stroke-width='1.5' stroke='currentColor' class='w-6 h-6'><path stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round' d='M6 18L18 6M6 6l12 12' /></svg>`,
		feed: `<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' viewBox='0 0 24 24' stroke-width='1.5' stroke='currentColor' class='w-5 h-5'><path stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round' d='M3.75 12h16.5m-16.5 3.75h16.5M3.75 19.5h16.5M5.625 4.5h12.75a1.875 1.875 0 010 3.75H5.625a1.875 1.875 0 010-3.75z' /></svg>`,
		materials: `<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' viewBox='0 0 24 24' stroke-width='1.5' stroke='currentColor' class='w-5 h-5'><path stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round' d='M20.25 7.5l-.625 10.632a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.247 2.118H6.622a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.247-2.118L3.75 7.5M10.5 11.25h3M12 15V3.75m0 0L10.5 5.25M12 3.75l1.5 1.5' /></svg>`
	};

	let processedNavLinks: NavLink[] = [];

	// Use a reactive block to ensure processedNavLinks is updated correctly
	// when currentAuth changes.
	$: {
		if (
			currentAuth &&
			currentAuth.isLoggedIn &&
			currentAuth.user &&
			typeof currentAuth.user.userType === 'string'
		) {
			const userType = currentAuth.user.userType;
			processedNavLinks = navLinks.filter((link) => {
				// Ensure link and its properties are valid before filtering
				if (link && typeof link.href === 'string' && Array.isArray(link.types)) {
					return link.types.includes('all') || link.types.includes(userType);
				}
				return false;
			});
		} else {
			processedNavLinks = [];
		}
		// console.log('[Layout Debug] currentAuth:', JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(currentAuth)));
		// console.log('[Layout Debug] processedNavLinks:', JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(processedNavLinks)));
	}
</script>

<div class="flex h-screen bg-slate-800 font-sans text-gray-100">
	<aside
		class:translate-x-0={sidebarOpen}
		class:!-translate-x-full={!sidebarOpen}

	>
		<div class="flex h-20 shrink-0 items-center justify-center border-b border-slate-700/50 px-4">

			</a>

    	<nav class="flex-grow space-y-2 overflow-y-auto p-4">
    		{#if currentAuth.isLoading && !currentAuth.isLoggedIn}
    			{#each Array(4) as _, index (index)}
    				<div class="mb-2 h-10 animate-pulse rounded-lg bg-slate-700/50"></div>
    			{/each}
    		{:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn && currentAuth.user}

                    {$page.url.pathname.startsWith(link.href) &&

    							? 'bg-emerald-600 text-white shadow-md'

    						{:else}

    						{/if}
    						<span class="truncate">{link.label}</span>
    					</a>
    				{/each}
    			{:else if !currentAuth.isLoading}
    				<!-- Show only if not loading and no links (which means userType might not match any specific links) -->
    				<p class="px-3 py-2.5 text-sm text-slate-400">
    					No specific navigation links for your role.
    				</p>
    			{/if}
    		{/if}
    	</nav>

    	<div class="shrink-0 border-t border-slate-700/50 p-4">
    		{#if currentAuth.isLoading && !currentAuth.isLoggedIn}
    			<div class="mb-3 flex animate-pulse items-center">
    				<div class="mr-3 h-10 w-10 rounded-full bg-slate-700"></div>
    				<div class="flex-1 space-y-2">
    					<div class="h-3 w-3/4 rounded bg-slate-700"></div>
    					<div class="h-2 w-1/2 rounded bg-slate-700"></div>
    					<div class="h-2 w-1/3 rounded bg-slate-700"></div>
    				</div>
    			</div>
    			<div class="h-9 animate-pulse rounded-lg bg-slate-700"></div>
    		{:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn && currentAuth.user}
    			<div class="mb-3 flex items-center">
    				<img
    					src={currentAuth.user.profile?.avatarUrl || '/default-avatar.png'}
    					alt={currentAuth.user.profile?.fullName || 'User Avatar'}
    					class="mr-3 h-10 w-10 shrink-0 rounded-full border-2 border-emerald-500 object-cover"
    					loading="lazy"
    				/>
    				<div class="overflow-hidden">
    					<p
    						class="truncate text-sm font-semibold text-white"
    						title={currentAuth.user.profile?.fullName || currentAuth.user.email.split('@')[0]}
    					>
    						{currentAuth.user.profile?.fullName || currentAuth.user.email.split('@')[0]}
    					</p>
    					<p class="truncate text-xs text-slate-400" title={currentAuth.user.email}>
    						{currentAuth.user.email}
    					</p>
    					<p class="text-xs text-amber-400 capitalize">{currentAuth.user.userType}</p>
    				</div>
    			</div>
    			<button
    				on:click={handleLogout}
    				class="flex w-full items-center justify-center space-x-2 rounded-lg bg-red-600 px-3 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-white transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out hover:bg-red-700"
    			>
    				{@html icons.logout}
    				<span>Logout</span>
    			</button>
    		{:else}
    			<a
    				href="/auth/login"
    				class="block w-full rounded-lg bg-emerald-500 px-4 py-2 text-center text-white hover:bg-emerald-600"
    			>
    				Login to Continue
    			</a>
    		{/if}
    	</div>
    </aside>

    <div class="flex flex-1 flex-col overflow-hidden">
    	<header

    	>

    		<button
    			on:click={() => (sidebarOpen = !sidebarOpen)}
    			class="-mr-2 p-2 text-slate-300 hover:text-emerald-400"
    		>
    			{#if sidebarOpen}
    				{@html icons.close}
    			{:else}

    			{/if}
    		</button>
    	</header>

    	<main class="flex-1 overflow-x-hidden overflow-y-auto bg-slate-800 p-4 sm:p-6 lg:p-8">
    		{#if currentAuth.isLoading}

    					></path>

    			</div>
    		{:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn && currentAuth.user}
    			<slot />
    		{:else}
    			<div class="flex h-full flex-col items-center justify-center text-center">
    				<h2 class="mb-3 text-2xl font-semibold text-sky-300">Access Denied</h2>

It appears I made a mistake in the previous edit of `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte`. I used Svelte 5 runes (`$derived` and `$state`) which are not compatible with your Svelte 4 setup. My apologies!

I need to revert those specific changes and use Svelte 4 compatible reactivity.

**Corrections to be made in `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte`:**

1.  **`currentAuth`**: Change `let currentAuth = $derived($authStore);` back to `$: currentAuth = $authStore;`.
2.  **`navLinks`**: Change `const navLinks: NavLink[] = $state([...]);` back to `const navLinks: NavLink[] = [...];` (it's a constant, so `$state` wasn't appropriate anyway).
3.  **`filteredNavLinks`**: Change `let filteredNavLinks = $derived(...)` back to `$: filteredNavLinks = navLinks.filter(...)`.
4.  **`sidebarOpen`**: Change `let sidebarOpen = $state(false);` back to `let sidebarOpen = false;`.

<script lang="ts">
	import { page } from '$app/stores';
	import { goto } from '$app/navigation';
	import { authStore, type AuthUser, type AuthState } from '$lib/stores/auth';
	// onMount is not strictly needed here anymore unless for specific onMount logic
	// import { onMount } from 'svelte';

	// Reactive subscription to the auth store
	// currentAuth will have the shape of AuthState from our auth.ts store
	// $: currentAuth = $authStore;
	let currentAuth = $derived($authStore);

	interface NavLink {
		href: string;
		label: string;
		iconKey: keyof typeof icons;
		types: Array<AuthUser['userType'] | 'all'>; // Use AuthUser['userType']
	}

	const navLinks: NavLink[] = $state([
		{ href: '/dashboard', label: 'Dashboard', iconKey: 'dashboard', types: ['all'] },
		{ href: '/chat', label: 'Chat', iconKey: 'chat', types: ['all'] },
		{ href: '/contracts', label: 'Contracts', iconKey: 'contracts', types: ['all'] },
		{
			href: '/customer/create-request',
			label: 'New Request',
			iconKey: 'newRequest',
			types: ['customer']
		}
		// Example of expert/supplier specific links (can be uncommented and icons added if needed)
		// { href: '/expert/work-feed', label: 'Work Feed', iconKey: 'feed', types: ['expert'] },
		// { href: '/supplier/material-requests', label: 'Material Feed', iconKey: 'materials', types: ['supplier'] },
	]);

	async function handleLogout() {
		await authStore.logout();
		// After logout, the authStore state will change, and the UI should react.
		// We also explicitly navigate to ensure the user lands on the login page.
		// A hook might also handle this redirection more globally.
		goto('/auth/login', { replaceState: true });
	}

	let sidebarOpen = $state(false);

	// SVG Icons
	const icons = {
		dashboard: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M3.75 6A2.25 2.25 0 016 3.75h2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0110.5 6v2.25a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25 2.25H6a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25-2.25V6zM3.75 15.75A2.25 2.25 0 016 13.5h2.25a2.25 2.25 0 012.25 2.25V18a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25 2.25H6a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25-2.25v-2.25zM13.5 6a2.25 2.25 0 012.25-2.25H18A2.25 2.25 0 0120.25 6v2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0118 10.5h-2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0113.5 8.25V6zM13.5 15.75a2.25 2.25 0 012.25-2.25H18a2.25 2.25 0 012.25 2.25V18A2.25 2.25 0 0118 20.25h-2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0113.5 18v-2.25z" /></svg>`,
		chat: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M20.25 8.511c.884.284 1.5 1.128 1.5 2.097v4.286c0 1.136-.847 2.1-1.98 2.193-.34.027-.68.052-1.02.072v3.091l-3.697-3.697c-.024-.024-.047-.048-.072-.071a11.91 11.91 0 01-6.185-1.347M16.5 9.75L9 14.25m7.5-4.5l-7.5 4.5M3.75 8.511c-.884.284-1.5 1.128-1.5 2.097v4.286c0 1.136.847 2.1 1.98 2.193.34.027-.68.052 1.02.072v3.091l3.697-3.697c.024-.024.047-.048-.072-.071a11.91 11.91 0 016.185-1.347m0-2.489c.331 0 .658.025.973.069a11.909 11.909 0 00-9.054-3.996c-1.756 0-3.404.44-4.84 1.206a11.892 11.892 0 00-6.184 3.99V10.5a2.25 2.25 0 012.25-2.25h13.5A2.25 2.25 0 0020.25 6v1.5a2.25 2.25 0 00-2.25 2.25H15m-1.5-1.5l-7.5 4.5" /></svg>`,
		contracts: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M19.5 14.25v-2.625a3.375 3.375 0 00-3.375-3.375h-1.5A1.125 1.125 0 0113.5 7.125v-1.5a3.375 3.375 0 00-3.375-3.375H8.25m0 12.75h7.5m-7.5 3H12M10.5 2.25H5.625c-.621 0-1.125.504-1.125 1.125v17.25c0 .621.504 1.125 1.125 1.125h12.75c.621 0 1.125-.504 1.125-1.125V11.25a9 9 0 00-9-9z" /></svg>`,
		newRequest: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M12 9v6m3-3H9m12 0a9 9 0 11-18 0 9 9 0 0118 0z" /></svg>`,
		logout: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M15.75 9V5.25A2.25 2.25 0 0013.5 3h-6a2.25 2.25 0 00-2.25 2.25v13.5A2.25 2.25 0 007.5 21h6a2.25 2.25 0 002.25-2.25V15M12 9l-3 3m0 0l3 3m-3-3h12.75" /></svg>`,
		menu: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-6 h-6"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M3.75 6.75h16.5M3.75 12h16.5m-16.5 5.25h16.5" /></svg>`,
		close: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-6 h-6"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M6 18L18 6M6 6l12 12" /></svg>`,
		feed: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M3.75 12h16.5m-16.5 3.75h16.5M3.75 19.5h16.5M5.625 4.5h12.75a1.875 1.875 0 010 3.75H5.625a1.875 1.875 0 010-3.75z" /></svg>`, // Placeholder
		materials: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M20.25 7.5l-.625 10.632a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.247 2.118H6.622a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.247-2.118L3.75 7.5M10.5 11.25h3M12 15V3.75m0 0L10.5 5.25M12 3.75l1.5 1.5" /></svg>` // Placeholder
	};

	// $: filteredNavLinks = navLinks.filter(
	// 	(link) =>
	// 		link.types.includes('all') ||
	// 		(currentAuth.user?.userType && link.types.includes(currentAuth.user.userType))
	// );

	let filteredNavLinks = $derived(
		navLinks.filter(
			(link) =>
				link.types.includes('all') ||
				(currentAuth.user?.userType && link.types.includes(currentAuth.user.userType))
		)
	);

	let { children } = $props(); // Svelte 4 way to get slot content
</script>

<div class="flex h-screen bg-slate-800 font-sans text-gray-100">
	<!-- Sidebar -->
	<aside
		class:translate-x-0={sidebarOpen}
		class:!-translate-x-full={!sidebarOpen}
		class="fixed inset-y-0 left-0 z-30 flex w-64 transform flex-col bg-slate-900 shadow-lg transition-transform duration-300 ease-in-out md:relative md:translate-x-0"
	>
		<!-- Logo -->
		<div class="flex h-20 shrink-0 items-center justify-center border-b border-slate-700/50 px-4">
			<a
				href="/dashboard"
				class="text-3xl font-bold text-emerald-400 transition-colors hover:text-emerald-300"
			>
				GEFIFI
			</a>
		</div>

    	<!-- Navigation Links -->
    	<nav class="flex-grow space-y-2 overflow-y-auto p-4">
    		{#if currentAuth.isLoading && !currentAuth.isLoggedIn}
    			<!-- Skeleton Loader for Nav when initially loading and not yet logged in -->
    			{#each Array(4) as index (index)}
    				<div class="mb-2 h-10 animate-pulse rounded-lg bg-slate-700/50"></div>
    			{/each}
    		{:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn && currentAuth.user}
    			{#each filteredNavLinks as link (link)}
    				<a
    					href={link.href}
    					class="group flex items-center space-x-3 rounded-lg px-3 py-2.5 transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out
                   {$page.url.pathname.startsWith(link.href) &&
    					(link.href !== '/dashboard' || $page.url.pathname === '/dashboard')
    						? 'bg-emerald-600 text-white shadow-md'
    						: 'text-slate-300 hover:bg-slate-700/50 hover:text-emerald-300'}"
    					onclick={() => (sidebarOpen = false)}
    					title={link.label}
    				>
    					{@html icons[link.iconKey]}
    					<span class="truncate">{link.label}</span>
    				</a>
    			{/each}
    		{/if}
    	</nav>

    	<!-- User Profile / Logout -->
    	<div class="shrink-0 border-t border-slate-700/50 p-4">
    		{#if currentAuth.isLoading && !currentAuth.isLoggedIn}
    			<div class="mb-3 flex animate-pulse items-center">
    				<div class="mr-3 h-10 w-10 rounded-full bg-slate-700"></div>
    				<div class="flex-1 space-y-2">
    					<div class="h-3 w-3/4 rounded bg-slate-700"></div>
    					<div class="h-2 w-1/2 rounded bg-slate-700"></div>
    					<div class="h-2 w-1/3 rounded bg-slate-700"></div>
    				</div>
    			</div>
    			<div class="h-9 animate-pulse rounded-lg bg-slate-700"></div>
    		{:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn && currentAuth.user}
    			<div class="mb-3 flex items-center">
    				<img
    					src={currentAuth.user.profile?.avatarUrl || '/default-avatar.png'}
    					alt={currentAuth.user.profile?.fullName || 'User Avatar'}
    					class="mr-3 h-10 w-10 shrink-0 rounded-full border-2 border-emerald-500 object-cover"
    					loading="lazy"
    				/>
    				<div class="overflow-hidden">
    					<p
    						class="truncate text-sm font-semibold text-white"
    						title={currentAuth.user.profile?.fullName}
    					>
    						{currentAuth.user.profile?.fullName || currentAuth.user.email.split('@')[0]}
    					</p>
    					<p class="truncate text-xs text-slate-400" title={currentAuth.user.email}>
    						{currentAuth.user.email}
    					</p>
    					<p class="text-xs text-amber-400 capitalize">{currentAuth.user.userType}</p>
    				</div>
    			</div>
    			<button
    				onclick={handleLogout}
    				class="flex w-full items-center justify-center space-x-2 rounded-lg bg-red-600 px-3 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-white transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out hover:bg-red-700"
    			>
    				{@html icons.logout}
    				<span>Logout</span>
    			</button>
    		{:else}
    			<!-- This case should ideally be handled by a route guard redirecting to login -->
    			<a
    				href="/auth/login"
    				class="block w-full rounded-lg bg-emerald-500 px-4 py-2 text-center text-white hover:bg-emerald-600"
    			>
    				Login to Continue
    			</a>
    		{/if}
    	</div>
    </aside>

    <!-- Main Content Area -->
    <div class="flex flex-1 flex-col overflow-hidden">
    	<!-- Mobile Header (for menu toggle) -->
    	<header
    		class="sticky top-0 z-20 flex h-16 shrink-0 items-center justify-between bg-slate-900 p-4 shadow-md md:hidden"
    	>
    		<a href="/dashboard" class="text-xl font-bold text-emerald-400">GEFIFI</a>
    		<button
    			onclick={() => (sidebarOpen = !sidebarOpen)}
    			class="-mr-2 p-2 text-slate-300 hover:text-emerald-400"
    		>
    			{#if sidebarOpen}
    				{@html icons.close}
    			{:else}
    				{@html icons.menu}
    			{/if}
    		</button>
    	</header>

    	<!-- Page Content Slot -->
    	<main class="flex-1 overflow-x-hidden overflow-y-auto bg-slate-800 p-4 sm:p-6 lg:p-8">
    		{#if currentAuth.isLoading}
    			<div class="flex h-full items-center justify-center">
    				<svg
    					class="h-8 w-8 animate-spin text-emerald-400"
    					xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
    					fill="none"
    					viewBox="0 0 24 24"
    				>
    					<circle class="opacity-25" cx="12" cy="12" r="10" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="4"
    					></circle>
    					<path
    						class="opacity-75"
    						fill="currentColor"
    						d="M4 12a8 8 0 018-8V0C5.373 0 0 5.373 0 12h4zm2 5.291A7.962 7.962 0 014 12H0c0 3.042 1.135 5.824 3 7.938l3-2.647z"
    					></path>
    				</svg>
    				<span class="ml-3 text-slate-300">Loading application state...</span>
    			</div>
    		{:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn}
    			{@render children()}
    		{:else}
    			<!-- This content is shown if not logged in.
             A route guard should ideally redirect to /auth/login instead of showing this in-place.
             The authStore.loadUserFromStorage() is called on app init.
             If it completes and isLoggedIn is false, a hook should redirect. -->
    			<div class="flex h-full flex-col items-center justify-center text-center">
    				<h2 class="mb-3 text-2xl font-semibold text-sky-300">Access Denied</h2>
    				<p class="mb-6 max-w-md text-slate-300">
    					It seems you're not logged in or your session might have expired. Please log in to
    					access your GEFIFI dashboard and features.
    				</p>
    				<a
    					href="/auth/login"
    					class="rounded-lg bg-emerald-500 px-8 py-3 font-semibold text-white shadow-md transition-colors hover:bg-emerald-600 hover:shadow-lg"
    				>
    					Go to Login
    				</a>
    			</div>
    		{/if}
    	</main>
    </div>

</div>

<!-- <style lang="postcss">
	@reference "../../app.css";
	/* Optional: Global scrollbar styling for a more consistent dark theme feel */
	/* These are webkit-specific but common */
	::-webkit-scrollbar {
		width: 8px;
		height: 8px;
	}
	::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
		@apply bg-slate-800; /* Or bg-slate-900 if sidebar is primary scroll context */
	}
	::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
		@apply rounded bg-slate-600;
	}
	::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
		@apply bg-emerald-500;
	}
</style> -->

<style lang="postcss">
	/* Optional: Global scrollbar styling for a more consistent dark theme feel */
	/* These are webkit-specific but common */
	::-webkit-scrollbar {
		width: 8px;
		height: 8px;
	}
	::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
		@reference bg-slate-800; /* Or bg-slate-900 if sidebar is primary scroll context */
	}
	::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
		@reference rounded bg-slate-600;
	}
	::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
		@reference bg-emerald-500;
	}
</style>

## Agent

You are absolutely right! It seems Svelte 5 syntax (runes like `$derived` and `$state`) crept into the `(app)/+layout.svelte` file, which is not what we intended for your Svelte 4 project. My apologies for that oversight.

Let's correct this to use standard Svelte 4 reactivity and slot handling.

**Key Corrections for Svelte 4 Compatibility:**

1.  **Reactivity**:
    - `let currentAuth = $derived($authStore);` will be changed to `$: currentAuth = $authStore;`
    - `const navLinks: NavLink[] = $state([...]);` will be changed to `const navLinks: NavLink[] = [...];` (since it's a top-level constant, `$state` is not applicable here).
    - `let filteredNavLinks = $derived(...)` will be changed to `$: filteredNavLinks = navLinks.filter(...)`.
    - `let sidebarOpen = $state(false);` will be changed to `let sidebarOpen = false;`.
2.  **Slot Handling**:
    - `let { children } = $props();` will be removed.
    - `{@render children()}` will be replaced with `<slot />`.
3.  **Keyed `each` block**:
    - Change `{#each filteredNavLinks as link (link)}` to `{#each filteredNavLinks as link (link.href)}` for a more stable and explicit key.
4.  **Styling with `@apply`**:
    - The `@reference` directive in the `<style lang="postcss">` block is incorrect for applying Tailwind classes. It should be `@apply`.

Here's the corrected version of `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte`:

```svelte
<!-- gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte -->
<script lang="ts">
	import { page } from '$app/stores';
	import { goto } from '$app/navigation';
	import { authStore, type AuthUser, type AuthState } from '$lib/stores/auth';

	// Reactive subscription to the auth store using Svelte 4 syntax
	$: currentAuth = $authStore;

	interface NavLink {
		href: string;
		label: string;
		iconKey: keyof typeof icons;
		types: Array<AuthUser['userType'] | 'all'>;
	}

	// navLinks is a constant array, no $state needed
	const navLinks: NavLink[] = [
		{ href: '/dashboard', label: 'Dashboard', iconKey: 'dashboard', types: ['all'] },
		{ href: '/chat', label: 'Chat', iconKey: 'chat', types: ['all'] },
		{ href: '/contracts', label: 'Contracts', iconKey: 'contracts', types: ['all'] },
		{
			href: '/customer/create-request',
			label: 'New Request',
			iconKey: 'newRequest',
			types: ['customer']
		}
		// Example of expert/supplier specific links
		// { href: '/expert/work-feed', label: 'Work Feed', iconKey: 'feed', types: ['expert'] },
		// { href: '/supplier/material-requests', label: 'Material Feed', iconKey: 'materials', types: ['supplier'] },
	];

	async function handleLogout() {
		await authStore.logout();
		goto('/auth/login', { replaceState: true });
	}

	// Regular Svelte 4 state variable
	let sidebarOpen = false;

	const icons = {
		dashboard: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M3.75 6A2.25 2.25 0 016 3.75h2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0110.5 6v2.25a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25 2.25H6a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25-2.25V6zM3.75 15.75A2.25 2.25 0 016 13.5h2.25a2.25 2.25 0 012.25 2.25V18a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25 2.25H6a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.25-2.25v-2.25zM13.5 6a2.25 2.25 0 012.25-2.25H18A2.25 2.25 0 0120.25 6v2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0118 10.5h-2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0113.5 8.25V6zM13.5 15.75a2.25 2.25 0 012.25-2.25H18a2.25 2.25 0 012.25 2.25V18A2.25 2.25 0 0118 20.25h-2.25A2.25 2.25 0 0113.5 18v-2.25z" /></svg>`,
		chat: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M20.25 8.511c.884.284 1.5 1.128 1.5 2.097v4.286c0 1.136-.847 2.1-1.98 2.193-.34.027-.68.052-1.02.072v3.091l-3.697-3.697c-.024-.024-.047-.048-.072-.071a11.91 11.91 0 01-6.185-1.347M16.5 9.75L9 14.25m7.5-4.5l-7.5 4.5M3.75 8.511c-.884.284-1.5 1.128-1.5 2.097v4.286c0 1.136.847 2.1 1.98 2.193.34.027-.68.052 1.02.072v3.091l3.697-3.697c.024-.024-.047-.048-.072-.071a11.91 11.91 0 016.185-1.347m0-2.489c.331 0 .658.025.973.069a11.909 11.909 0 00-9.054-3.996c-1.756 0-3.404.44-4.84 1.206a11.892 11.892 0 00-6.184 3.99V10.5a2.25 2.25 0 012.25-2.25h13.5A2.25 2.25 0 0020.25 6v1.5a2.25 2.25 0 00-2.25 2.25H15m-1.5-1.5l-7.5 4.5\" /></svg>`,
		contracts: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M19.5 14.25v-2.625a3.375 3.375 0 00-3.375-3.375h-1.5A1.125 1.125 0 0113.5 7.125v-1.5a3.375 3.375 0 00-3.375-3.375H8.25m0 12.75h7.5m-7.5 3H12M10.5 2.25H5.625c-.621 0-1.125.504-1.125 1.125v17.25c0 .621.504 1.125 1.125 1.125h12.75c.621 0 1.125-.504 1.125-1.125V11.25a9 9 0 00-9-9z\" /></svg>`,
		newRequest: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M12 9v6m3-3H9m12 0a9 9 0 11-18 0 9 9 0 0118 0z\" /></svg>`,
		logout: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M15.75 9V5.25A2.25 2.25 0 0013.5 3h-6a2.25 2.25 0 00-2.25 2.25v13.5A2.25 2.25 0 007.5 21h6a2.25 2.25 0 002.25-2.25V15M12 9l-3 3m0 0l3 3m-3-3h12.75\" /></svg>`,
		menu: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-6 h-6"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M3.75 6.75h16.5M3.75 12h16.5m-16.5 5.25h16.5\" /></svg>`,
		close: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-6 h-6"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M6 18L18 6M6 6l12 12\" /></svg>`,
		feed: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M3.75 12h16.5m-16.5 3.75h16.5M3.75 19.5h16.5M5.625 4.5h12.75a1.875 1.875 0 010 3.75H5.625a1.875 1.875 0 010-3.75z\" /></svg>`,
		materials: `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke-width="1.5" stroke="currentColor" class="w-5 h-5"><path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M20.25 7.5l-.625 10.632a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.247 2.118H6.622a2.25 2.25 0 01-2.247-2.118L3.75 7.5M10.5 11.25h3M12 15V3.75m0 0L10.5 5.25M12 3.75l1.5 1.5\" /></svg>`
	};

	// Svelte 4 reactive declaration for filteredNavLinks
	$: filteredNavLinks = navLinks.filter(
		(link) =>
			link.types.includes('all') ||
			(currentAuth.user?.userType && link.types.includes(currentAuth.user.userType))
	);

	// Svelte 4 does not use $props() for slot content in this way.
	// The <slot /> tag is used directly in the template.
</script>

<div class="flex h-screen bg-slate-800 font-sans text-gray-100">
	<!-- Sidebar -->
	<aside
		class:translate-x-0={sidebarOpen}
		class:!-translate-x-full={!sidebarOpen}
		class="fixed inset-y-0 left-0 z-30 flex w-64 transform flex-col bg-slate-900 shadow-lg transition-transform duration-300 ease-in-out md:relative md:translate-x-0"
	>
		<!-- Logo -->
		<div class="flex h-20 shrink-0 items-center justify-center border-b border-slate-700/50 px-4">
			<a
				href="/dashboard"
				class="text-3xl font-bold text-emerald-400 transition-colors hover:text-emerald-300"
			>
				GEFIFI
			</a>
		</div>

		<!-- Navigation Links -->
		<nav class="flex-grow space-y-2 overflow-y-auto p-4">
			{#if currentAuth.isLoading && !currentAuth.isLoggedIn}
				<!-- Skeleton Loader for Nav -->
				{#each Array(4) as _, index (index)}
					<!-- Added key for Svelte 4 best practice -->
					<div class="mb-2 h-10 animate-pulse rounded-lg bg-slate-700/50"></div>
				{/each}
			{:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn && currentAuth.user}
				{#each filteredNavLinks as link (link.href)}
					<!-- Use link.href as key -->
					<a
						href={link.href}
						class="group flex items-center space-x-3 rounded-lg px-3 py-2.5 transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out
                   {$page.url.pathname.startsWith(link.href) &&
						(link.href !== '/dashboard' || $page.url.pathname === '/dashboard')
							? 'bg-emerald-600 text-white shadow-md'
							: 'text-slate-300 hover:bg-slate-700/50 hover:text-emerald-300'}"
						on:click={() => (sidebarOpen = false)}
						title={link.label}
					>
						{#if icons[link.iconKey]}
							{@html icons[link.iconKey]}
						{:else}
							<span class="inline-block h-5 w-5"></span> <!-- Fallback for missing icon -->
						{/if}
						<span class="truncate">{link.label}</span>
					</a>
				{/each}
			{/if}
		</nav>

		<!-- User Profile / Logout -->
		<div class="shrink-0 border-t border-slate-700/50 p-4">
			{#if currentAuth.isLoading && !currentAuth.isLoggedIn}
				<div class="mb-3 flex animate-pulse items-center">
					<div class="mr-3 h-10 w-10 rounded-full bg-slate-700"></div>
					<div class="flex-1 space-y-2">
						<div class="h-3 w-3/4 rounded bg-slate-700"></div>
						<div class="h-2 w-1/2 rounded bg-slate-700"></div>
						<div class="h-2 w-1/3 rounded bg-slate-700"></div>
					</div>
				</div>
				<div class="h-9 animate-pulse rounded-lg bg-slate-700"></div>
			{:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn && currentAuth.user}
				<div class="mb-3 flex items-center">
					<img
						src={currentAuth.user.profile?.avatarUrl || '/default-avatar.png'}
						alt={currentAuth.user.profile?.fullName || 'User Avatar'}
						class="mr-3 h-10 w-10 shrink-0 rounded-full border-2 border-emerald-500 object-cover"
						loading="lazy"
					/>
					<div class="overflow-hidden">
						<p
							class="truncate text-sm font-semibold text-white"
							title={currentAuth.user.profile?.fullName || currentAuth.user.email.split('@')[0]}
						>
							{currentAuth.user.profile?.fullName || currentAuth.user.email.split('@')[0]}
						</p>
						<p class="truncate text-xs text-slate-400" title={currentAuth.user.email}>
							{currentAuth.user.email}
						</p>
						<p class="text-xs text-amber-400 capitalize">{currentAuth.user.userType}</p>
					</div>
				</div>
				<button
					on:click={handleLogout}
					class="flex w-full items-center justify-center space-x-2 rounded-lg bg-red-600 px-3 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-white transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out hover:bg-red-700"
				>
					{@html icons.logout}
					<span>Logout</span>
				</button>
			{:else}
				<a
					href="/auth/login"
					class="block w-full rounded-lg bg-emerald-500 px-4 py-2 text-center text-white hover:bg-emerald-600"
				>
					Login to Continue
				</a>
			{/if}
		</div>
	</aside>

	<!-- Main Content Area -->
	<div class="flex flex-1 flex-col overflow-hidden">
		<!-- Mobile Header (for menu toggle) -->
		<header
			class="sticky top-0 z-20 flex h-16 shrink-0 items-center justify-between bg-slate-900 p-4 shadow-md md:hidden"
		>
			<a href="/dashboard" class="text-xl font-bold text-emerald-400">GEFIFI</a>
			<button
				on:click={() => (sidebarOpen = !sidebarOpen)}
				class="-mr-2 p-2 text-slate-300 hover:text-emerald-400"
			>
				{#if sidebarOpen}
					{@html icons.close}
				{:else}
					{@html icons.menu}
				{/if}
			</button>
		</header>

		<!-- Page Content Slot -->
		<main class="flex-1 overflow-x-hidden overflow-y-auto bg-slate-800 p-4 sm:p-6 lg:p-8">
			{#if currentAuth.isLoading}
				<div class="flex h-full items-center justify-center">
					<svg
						class="h-8 w-8 animate-spin text-emerald-400"
						xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
						fill="none"
						viewBox="0 0 24 24"
					>
						<circle class="opacity-25" cx="12" cy="12" r="10" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="4"
						></circle>
						<path
							class="opacity-75"
							fill="currentColor"
							d="M4 12a8 8 0 018-8V0C5.373 0 0 5.373 0 12h4zm2 5.291A7.962 7.962 0 014 12H0c0 3.042 1.135 5.824 3 7.938l3-2.647z"
						></path>
					</svg>
					<span class="ml-3 text-slate-300">Loading application state...</span>
				</div>
			{:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn}
				<slot /> <!-- Svelte 4 default slot -->
			{:else}
				<div class="flex h-full flex-col items-center justify-center text-center">
					<h2 class="mb-3 text-2xl font-semibold text-sky-300">Access Denied</h2>
					<p class="mb-6 max-w-md text-slate-300">
						It seems you're not logged in or your session might have expired. Please log in to
						access your GEFIFI dashboard and features.
					</p>
					<a
						href="/auth/login"
						class="rounded-lg bg-emerald-500 px-8 py-3 font-semibold text-white shadow-md transition-colors hover:bg-emerald-600 hover:shadow-lg"
					>
						Go to Login
					</a>
				</div>
			{/if}
		</main>
	</div>
</div>

<style lang="postcss">
	/* Optional: Global scrollbar styling for a more consistent dark theme feel */
	::-webkit-scrollbar {
		width: 8px;
		height: 8px;
	}
	::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
		@apply bg-slate-800; /* Or bg-slate-900 if sidebar is primary scroll context */
	}
	::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
		@apply rounded bg-slate-600;
	}
	::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
		@apply bg-emerald-500;
	}
</style>
```

I've reverted the Svelte 5 specific syntax to Svelte 4 compatible code:

- `$derived` and `$state` have been removed.
- Reactivity is handled with `$: ...` for `currentAuth` and `filteredNavLinks`.
- `sidebarOpen` is now a standard `let` variable.
- The `$props()` and `{@render children()}` have been replaced with the standard `<slot />` for Svelte 4.
- Added explicit key `(link.href)` to the main navigation `{#each}` loop.
- Added a fallback for icons in case an `iconKey` is somehow missing.
- Corrected `@reference` to `@apply` in the `<style>` block.

I will now apply these fixes to `gefifi-2/src/frontend/src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte`.

**Use Tool: edit_file (edit_file-2)**

    viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n          >\n            <circle class=\"opacity-25\" cx=\"12\" cy=\"12\" r=\"10\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"4\"\n            ></circle>\n            <path\n 7.938l3-2.647z\"\n            ></path>\n          </svg>\n          <span class=\"ml-3 text-slate-300\">Loading application state...</span>\n        </div>\n      {:else if currentAuth.isLoggedIn}\n        <slot /> <!-- Svelte 4 default slot -->\n      {:else}\n        <div class=\"flex h-full flex-col items-center justify-center text-center\">\n          <h2 class=\"mb-3 text-2xl font-semibold text-sky-300\">Access Denied</h2>\n          <p class=\"mb-6 max-w-md text-slate-300\">\n            It seems you're not logged in or your session might have expired. Please log in to\n            access your GEFIFI dashboard and features.\n          </p>\n          <a\n            href=\"/auth/login\"\n            class=\"rounded-lg bg-emerald-500 px-8 py-3 font-semibold text-white shadow-md transition-colors hover:bg-emerald-600 hover:shadow-lg\"\n          >\n            Go to Login\n          </a>\n        </div>\n      {/if}\n    </main>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<style lang=\"postcss\">\n  /* Optional: Global scrollbar styling for a more consistent dark theme feel */\n  ::-webkit-scrollbar {\n    width: 8px;\n    height: 8px;\n  }\n  ::-webkit-scrollbar-track {\n    @apply bg-slate-800; /* Or bg-slate-900 if sidebar is primary scroll context */\n  }\n  ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {\n    @apply rounded bg-slate-600;\n  }\n  ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {\n    @apply bg-emerald-500;\n  }\n</style>\n",

}

-
 <style lang="postcss">
-	/* Optional: Global scrollbar styling for a more consistent dark theme feel */
-	/* These are webkit-specific but common */
-	::-webkit-scrollbar {
-		width: 8px;
-		height: 8px;
-	}
-	::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
-		@reference bg-slate-800; /* Or bg-slate-900 if sidebar is primary scroll context */
-	}
-	::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
-		@reference rounded bg-slate-600;
-	}
-	::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
-		@reference bg-emerald-500;
-	}
+  /* Optional: Global scrollbar styling for a more consistent dark theme feel */
+  ::-webkit-scrollbar {
+    width: 8px;
+    height: 8px;
+  }
+  ::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
+    @apply bg-slate-800; /* Or bg-slate-900 if sidebar is primary scroll context */
+  }
+  ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
+    @apply rounded bg-slate-600;
+  }
+  ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
+    @apply bg-emerald-500;
+  }
 </style>
